schema.name: HasResolutionPlan
schema.Description: TRUE when someone has written down how this gap would be closed.

schema.name: IsAbandonedUnknown
schema.Description: TRUE when an overdue gap has either no plan or no living owner — an admission of ignorance that nobody is acting on.

schema.name: OpenBlockingGapVersionKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: this gap's procedure version when the gap is both open and blocking, blank otherwise.

schema.name: OwnerRoleIsVacated
schema.Description: Whether the role that owns this gap is currently vacated.

schema.name: IsOwnerlessOpenGap
schema.Description: An open gap whose owning role nobody currently holds — an acknowledged unknown with nobody accountable for closing it.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI.

data.Statement: No validated fallback exists for simultaneous ERP and signed-snapshot repository outage.
data.ResolutionPlan: Resolved by adding offline signed export escrow.

data.Statement: SMS provider delivery receipts are not always final for ported numbers.
req-close-separation stated the preparer may not be the final approver, but nothing evaluated it. The control existed only as prose in a Requirements row, so a violation would have been invisible.
data.ResolutionPlan: Resolved by StepExecutions.ViolatesSeparationOfDuties, which counts preparation steps in the same procedure execution performed by the agent now approving. Verified by staging a breach (maria-chen approving se-close05 after preparing se-close03): the witness read TRUE. The staged breach was reverted; the control now reads FALSE because separation genuinely holds, not because nothing is checking.

data.Statement: StewardshipAssignments promised a 90-day review cadence for close-v1.1.0 and 60 days for policy-v1.0.0. Both the promise and the actual review history were in the model and nothing compared them, so a lapsed cadence was invisible.
data.ResolutionPlan: Resolved by ReviewEvents.ExceedsPromisedCadence and PromiseAndBehaviorDisagree, which compare DaysSinceReviewed against the cadence promised by the version's stewardship assignment. Surfacing the real Q1 review (review-close-q1) showed the witness firing on genuine history: 121 days elapsed against a 90-day promise. The Q2 review closed the lapse, so current state reads compliant.

data.Statement: Five of twelve roles were held only by a denormalized Roles.CurrentAgent pointer with no RoleAssignments row: communications-manager, knowledge-authority, process-steward, close-automation, policy-drafting-agent. The convenience pointer claimed a holder the auditable time-bounded table could not confirm, so provenance for anything those roles touched was unverifiable.
data.ResolutionPlan: Surfaced by Agents.IsStillEngaged, which counts CURRENT RoleAssignments rather than trusting Roles.CurrentAgent. amina-yusuf read as not engaged, which made kf-policy-channel an IsUndefendableTacitClaim. Resolved by recording the five missing time-bounded assignments. The witness stays: it is the only thing that distinguishes a real appointment from a denormalized pointer.

data.Statement: comm-sms-policy set ConsentRequired=true and exc-no-sms-consent documented the suppress-and-email handling, but no per-recipient delivery record existed, so a send to a non-consenting recipient was structurally invisible. req-policy-consent was a blocking control with zero satisfaction records.
data.ResolutionPlan: Resolved by the Recipients and MessageDeliveries tables plus MessageDeliveries.IsConsentViolation. Proven on md-003, a real SMS to a never-consented recipient, which the witness flags while correctly NOT flagging md-004 (consent revoked, properly suppressed under exc-no-sms-consent) or md-006 (unreachable, failed, HR case opened). Recipient re-consented; the delivery row is retained as evidence.

data.Statement: comm-sms-policy defined a 20:00-08:00 quiet window and nothing compared a send time against it.
data.ResolutionPlan: Resolved by MessageDeliveries.IsInsideQuietWindow and IsQuietHoursViolation, proven on md-005 (21:00 send). QuietHoursStartHour is stored rather than parsed: VALUE(LEFT("20:00",2)) does not translate — the transpiler casts the string to a timestamp and the view errors.

data.Statement: MessageTemplates carried Status="Approved" as a bare string with no approval record behind it, and nothing evaluated a send BEFORE transmission. Every gate the communication policies define — consent, quiet hours, length, opt-out, approval — was checkable only after the fact, if at all.
data.ResolutionPlan: Resolved by the SendIntents table and IsClearedToSend, which gates a proposed send on permission, timing, content, and authorization before it happens, and names the blocking gate. Proven across seven intents: Timing blocks a 21:00 send, Content blocks an oversized body and one missing the STOP phrase, Permission blocks an unreachable recipient. Authorization blocked ALL sends until tmpl-policy-sms received a real approval record (tap-sms-001) — the pipeline had been transmitting under a template nobody had attributably approved.

FAQs.Description: Frequently asked procedural questions. Maps to pko:FrequentlyAskedQuestion, question, answer, hasFAQCategory, and hasFAQTarget.

schema.name: ProcedureVersion

schema.name: Step
schema.Description: Optional step targeted by the FAQ.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: FAQ category.

schema.name: TargetKind
schema.Description: Procedure, Step, Tool, Resource, or Execution.

schema.name: Question

schema.name: Answer
schema.Description: Approved answer; maps to pko:answer.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.Question: Where are close workpapers stored?
data.Answer: In the controlled close evidence repository linked from the execution record; email attachments are not the system of record.

data.Question: Can the drafting AI approve a workforce policy?
data.Answer: No. It may draft from approved sources, but a human policy owner and legal reviewer must approve the commitments.

data.Question: What happens when an employee opts out of SMS?
data.Answer: The pipeline suppresses SMS and continues through the approved email channel.

Explanations.Description: Explainable derivation artifacts associated with procedural decisions. Maps to pko:Explanation and pko:hasExplanation.

schema.name: ExplanationId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one Explanations row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the Explanations row.

schema.name: ProcedureVersion
schema.Description: Procedure version explained.

schema.name: Step
schema.Description: Step explained.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Explanation title.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What the explanation traces.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.Title: Variance escalation explanation
data.Description: Explains the derived path from source timestamps, materiality, recurrence, and anomaly classification to the escalation decision.

data.Title: Channel eligibility explanation
data.Description: Explains why each recipient received email, SMS, both, or an exception task based on consent and channel policy.

ProcedureExecutions.Description: Concrete enactments of procedure specifications. Maps to pko:ProcedureExecution and remains separate from ProcedureVersions.

schema.name: ProcedureExecutionId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one ProcedureExecutions row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the ProcedureExecutions row.

schema.name: ProcedureVersion
schema.Description: Exact procedure specification executed; maps to pko:hasExecutedProcedure.

schema.name: ExecutionStatus
schema.Description: PKO execution status: InProgress, Completed, Paused, or Cancelled.

schema.name: StartedAt
schema.Description: Execution start; maps to prov:startedAtTime.

schema.name: EndedAt
schema.Description: Execution end; maps to prov:endedAtTime.

schema.name: ExecutedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent accountable for the overall execution; maps to pko:wasExecutedBy.

schema.name: Context
schema.Description: Execution-specific scope or target.

schema.name: OperationalRecordUri
schema.Description: Identifier in the operational execution system.

schema.name: ExpectedStepCount
schema.Description: How many steps this execution was supposed to perform.

schema.name: CompletedStepCount
schema.Description: How many steps of this execution actually reached Completed.

schema.name: ControlBreachCount
schema.Description: Total step executions in this run that carry at least one control breach.

schema.name: LateStepCount
schema.Description: How many steps in this execution ran long.

schema.name: IsStructurallyComplete
schema.Description: TRUE when every specified step of the procedure version reached Completed in this execution.

schema.name: DivergedFromSpecification
schema.Description: TRUE when the execution either skipped specified steps or carried at least one control breach.

schema.name: AllBlockingControlsEvaluated
schema.Description: TRUE when every blocking requirement bound to every step of this execution received a satisfaction record.

schema.name: UnevaluatedBlockingTotal
schema.Description: How many step executions in this run left at least one blocking control unevaluated.

schema.name: SeparationOfDutiesHeld
schema.Description: TRUE when no agent both prepared and approved within this execution.

schema.name: SeparationViolationCount
schema.Description: Number of segregation-of-duties violations in this execution.

schema.name: IsAttestationReady
schema.Description: TRUE only when every step completed, no control breached, every blocking control was actually evaluated, and segregation of duties held.

schema.name: AttestationBlockerSummary
schema.Description: Names the highest-severity reason the attestation cannot be signed, or empty when it can.
schema.formula: =IF({{IsAttestationReady}}, "", IF(NOT({{IsStructurallyComplete}}), "Incomplete: specified steps did not all complete.", IF({{SeparationViolationCount}} > 0, "Segregation of duties violated.", IF({{UnevaluatedBlockingTotal}} > 0, "Blocking controls were never evaluated.", "Control breach recorded on one or more steps."))))

schema.name: ExecutedVersionIsFit
schema.Description: Whether the procedure version this execution ran against is currently fit to execute.

schema.name: SignedAgainstUnfitVersion
schema.Description: TRUE when a completed execution was run against a procedure version the organization no longer stands behind.

schema.name: AssertedOnlyControlCount
schema.Description: How many blocking controls in this execution passed on human assertion alone.

schema.name: AssuranceIsMostlyAsserted
schema.Description: TRUE when any blocking control in this execution passed without a computed witness behind it.

schema.name: UnreachableHandlingFailureCount
schema.Description: Number of deliveries in this execution where unreachable handling was wrong -- either fabricated acknowledgement or no exception invoked.

schema.name: RetentionBreachCount
schema.Description: Number of transmitted messages in this execution whose text we no longer hold despite an active retention obligation.

schema.name: ClearedLegalReviewCount
schema.Description: How many passed legal reviews exist for this execution.

schema.name: HasClearedLegalReview
schema.Description: TRUE when legal review has passed for this execution.

schema.name: AbandonedFailureCount
schema.Description: Number of failed deliveries in this run that nobody picked up.

schema.name: DeliveredCount
schema.Description: Number of confirmed-delivered messages in this run.

schema.name: TotalDeliveryAttemptCount
schema.Description: Total delivery records of any status for this run.

schema.name: HasAbandonedFailures
schema.Description: TRUE when this run has any untriaged delivery failure.

schema.name: MishandledRefusalCount
schema.Description: Number of gate refusals in this run that were either overridden or silently dropped.

schema.name: UncleanStepCount
schema.Description: Number of step executions in this run that were not clean.

schema.name: RanClean
schema.Description: TRUE when every step execution in this run was clean.

schema.name: CountOfApprovalExecutions
schema.Description: How many human approval gates were executed in this run.

schema.name: HasHumanApproval
schema.Description: TRUE when at least one human approval gate was executed in this run.

schema.name: CountOfDeliveryExecutions
schema.Description: How many times the send step ran in this execution.

schema.name: HasDelivered
schema.Description: TRUE when this run has sent communications to employees.

schema.name: DeliveredWithoutApproval
schema.Description: TRUE when a run sent employee communications without executing a human approval gate.

schema.name: InvalidApprovalCount
schema.Description: Number of approval-type requirements in this run that are incomplete or non-human-evaluated.

schema.name: ApprovalChainIsComplete
schema.Description: TRUE when every approval-type requirement in this run is fully satisfied by a human.

schema.name: VacuouslyCleanStepCount
schema.Description: How many steps in this run were clean only because nothing checked them.

schema.name: PreparationStepCount
schema.Description: How many preparation steps this execution actually ran.

schema.name: ApprovalStepCount
schema.Description: How many approval steps this execution actually ran.

schema.name: SeparationWasTestable
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution contained both a preparation step and an approval step, so segregation of duties had an opportunity to fail.

schema.name: SeparationHeldUnderTest
schema.Description: TRUE only when segregation of duties both could have failed and did not.

schema.name: SeparationIsVacuouslyGreen
schema.Description: TRUE when the segregation control reports as held on a run where it could not have failed.

schema.name: SeparationAssuranceNote
schema.Description: The sentence that goes into the control narrative for this execution.
schema.formula: =IF({{SeparationViolationCount}} > 0, "Violated: same agent prepared and approved.", IF({{SeparationIsVacuouslyGreen}}, "Not tested: this run had no preparation/approval pair.", "Held under test."))

schema.name: UngovernedDivergenceCount
schema.Description: How many steps in this run departed from spec with nothing authorising the departure.

schema.name: DivergenceWasFullyGoverned
schema.Description: TRUE when this run departed from specification and every departure was authorised.

schema.name: ComputedlyWitnessedControlCount
schema.Description: How many blocking controls in this run were cleared by something computed.

schema.name: EvaluatedControlCount
schema.Description: Total blocking controls actually evaluated in this run, computed and asserted together.

schema.name: ComputedAssuranceRatio
schema.Description: The fraction of evaluated blocking controls in this run that rest on a computed witness rather than a human assertion.

schema.name: InterestedPartyAssertionCount
schema.Description: How many controls in this run were cleared by assertion from someone with an interest in the outcome.

schema.name: AssuranceGrade
schema.Description: Names the quality of the assurance behind this execution, worst case first.
schema.formula: =IF({{EvaluatedControlCount}} = 0, "None: no blocking control was evaluated.", IF({{InterestedPartyAssertionCount}} > 0, "Weak: at least one control rests on an interested-party assertion.", IF({{ComputedAssuranceRatio}} < 0.5, "Thin: most controls rest on human assertion.", IF({{ComputedAssuranceRatio}} < 1, "Mixed: computed and asserted controls.", "Computed: every evaluated control has a witness."))))

schema.name: AttestationWouldBeWeaklyBased
schema.Description: TRUE when the model says I may sign, but the basis for that permission is mostly or partly unwitnessed assertion.

schema.name: IndependentHumanObservationCount
schema.Description: How many verifications in this run were independently observed by a human with evidence attached.

schema.name: HasAnyIndependentObservation
schema.Description: TRUE when at least one verification in this run was independently observed by a human.

schema.name: SelfAttestedApprovalCount
schema.Description: How many approval steps in this run rested on self-attestation.

schema.name: AssuranceChainIsCircular
schema.Description: TRUE when every approval in this run rests on self-attestation and no independent human observation exists anywhere in it.

schema.name: LatestAttestationInstant
schema.Description: The most recent signature instant for this execution.

schema.name: HasBeenAttested
schema.Description: TRUE when someone has signed for this execution.

schema.name: AttestationCount
schema.Description: How many signatures exist against this execution.

schema.name: PostAttestationScoreCount
schema.Description: How many controls were scored after this execution was signed.

schema.name: BasisChangedAfterSignature
schema.Description: TRUE when controls this attestation depended on were scored after the signature was given.

schema.name: RequiresReAttestation
schema.Description: TRUE when the basis changed after signature AND the execution no longer reads as attestable.

schema.name: IntendedRecipientCount
schema.Description: How many sends this campaign proposed to make.

schema.name: ReachedRecipientCount
schema.Description: How many of those sends actually left our systems.

schema.name: SilentlyDroppedCount
schema.Description: How many intended recipients disappeared from this campaign with no record.

schema.name: DeliveryYieldPercent
schema.Description: Percentage of intended recipients who actually received the communication.

schema.name: CampaignSilentlyLostAudience
schema.Description: TRUE when a campaign lost intended recipients without producing any record of the loss.

schema.name: UnrecordedRefusalCount
schema.Description: How many sends this execution refused without recording the refusal anywhere.

schema.name: HasUnrecordedRefusals
schema.Description: TRUE when this run contains at least one refusal that left no trace anywhere.

schema.name: IndependentlyConfirmedIntentCount
schema.Description: How many of this execution's send decisions were corroborated by a delivery record.

schema.name: SendDecisionsAreEntirelySelfWitnessed
schema.Description: TRUE when no send decision in this run was confirmed by anything other than the pipeline itself.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

StepExecutions.Description: Concrete executions of specified steps. Maps to pko:StepExecution, hasExecutedStep, includesStepExecution, and nextStepExecution.

schema.name: StepExecutionId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one StepExecutions row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the StepExecutions row.

schema.name: ProcedureExecution
schema.Description: Parent procedure execution.

schema.name: Step
schema.Description: Specified step executed; maps to pko:hasExecutedStep.

schema.name: ExecutedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent executing the step; maps to pko:wasExecutedBy.

schema.name: ExecutionStatus
schema.Description: InProgress, Completed, Paused, Cancelled, or Failed.

schema.name: StartedAt
schema.Description: Execution start.

schema.name: EndedAt
schema.Description: Execution end.

schema.name: VerificationResult
schema.Description: PASS, WARN, FAIL, or PENDING.

schema.name: Deviation
schema.Description: Observed deviation from the specification.

schema.name: ActualDurationMinutes
schema.Description: Observed duration in minutes.

schema.name: ExpectedDurationMinutes
schema.Description: Expected duration from the specification.

schema.name: IsLate
schema.Description: TRUE when actual duration exceeds expected duration.

schema.name: BlockingUnmetCount
schema.Description: How many blocking requirements attached to this step execution are recorded as not fully satisfied.

schema.name: BlockingUnmetCountSafe
schema.Description: Count of blocking-but-unmet requirements on this step execution, computed via the single-criterion key so no criterion is dropped.

schema.name: ProceededPastBlockingControl
schema.Description: TRUE when a step execution reached Completed even though a blocking requirement on it was never fully satisfied.

schema.name: ExpectedBlockingCount
schema.Description: How many blocking requirements this execution's step was supposed to have evaluated.

schema.name: EvaluatedBlockingCount
schema.Description: How many blocking requirements actually received a satisfaction record on this step execution, at any level.

schema.name: UnevaluatedBlockingCount
schema.Description: Blocking requirements that were bound to this step but never assessed on this execution.

schema.name: HasUnevaluatedBlockingControl
schema.Description: TRUE when at least one blocking control bound to this step was never evaluated on this execution.

schema.name: StaleAuthoritativeSourceCount
schema.Description: Number of stale authoritative sources bound to the step this execution ran.

schema.name: RanOnStaleAuthoritativeSource
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution's step depends on at least one authoritative source that is outside its freshness SLA.

schema.name: HasDeviationNote
schema.Description: TRUE when a human recorded some deviation narrative on this execution.

schema.name: IsLateAndUnexplained
schema.Description: TRUE when the execution exceeded its expected duration and no deviation was recorded.

schema.name: AvailableExceptionCountForStep
schema.Description: How many active exceptions were available to this execution's step.

schema.name: HadUninvokedExceptionAvailable
schema.Description: TRUE when an execution ran long with no explanation despite the specification defining an active exception for exactly that situation.

schema.name: ExpectedVerificationCount
schema.Description: How many verifications this execution was supposed to perform.

schema.name: PerformedVerificationCount
schema.Description: How many verification outcomes were actually recorded against this execution.

schema.name: SkippedVerificationCount
schema.Description: Declared verifications with no recorded outcome on this execution.

schema.name: HasSkippedVerification
schema.Description: TRUE when a declared verification was never performed on this execution.

schema.name: ClaimsPassWithoutEvidence
schema.Description: TRUE when an execution asserts PASS while at least one declared verification has no recorded outcome.

schema.name: StepIsPreparation
schema.Description: Whether this execution ran a preparation step.

schema.name: StepIsApproval
schema.Description: Whether this execution ran an approval step.

schema.name: PreparerAgentKey
schema.Description: Composite execution+agent key, emitted only for preparation steps.

schema.name: ApproverAgentKey
schema.Description: Composite execution+agent key, emitted only for approval steps.

schema.name: PreparedByThisAgentCount
schema.Description: On an approval row, how many preparation steps in the SAME procedure execution were run by this same agent.

schema.name: ViolatesSeparationOfDuties
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent approving this step also prepared work earlier in the same procedure execution.

schema.name: RequiredRoleForStep
schema.Description: The role the specification assigns to this step.

schema.name: ExecutorRoleKey
schema.Description: The agent+role pair that would need to exist as a valid assignment for this execution to be properly authorized.

schema.name: ExecutorAuthorityCount
schema.Description: How many currently-valid role assignments grant this executor the role their step required.

schema.name: ExecutorHeldRequiredRole
schema.Description: TRUE when the executing agent holds a currently-valid assignment to the role the step required.

schema.name: IsUnauthorizedApproval
schema.Description: TRUE when an approval step was executed by an agent who does not hold the required approving role.

schema.name: CompletedExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id only for completed steps.

schema.name: ControlBreachExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step execution carries ANY control breach.

schema.name: LateExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id only for steps that ran past their expected duration.

schema.name: ExecutorAgentKind
schema.Description: Human, AIAgent, or AutomatedPipeline — what kind of agent actually ran this step.

schema.name: ExecutorIsHuman
schema.Description: TRUE when a human executed this step.

schema.name: StepRequiresHumanConfirmation
schema.Description: Whether the specification requires this step to be human-confirmed.

schema.name: NonHumanRanHumanStep
schema.Description: TRUE when a step requiring human confirmation was executed by an AI agent or automated pipeline.

schema.name: NonHumanApproval
schema.Description: TRUE when an approval-authority step was executed by a non-human agent, regardless of the RequiresHumanConfirmation flag.

schema.name: UnevaluatedBlockingExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step left a blocking control unevaluated.

schema.name: SeparationViolationExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step execution violates segregation of duties.

schema.name: SelfWitnessedVerificationCount
schema.Description: How many of this execution's verifications were observed by the same agent who ran the step.

schema.name: UnbackedVerificationCount
schema.Description: How many verifications on this execution recorded a matching signal with no retained evidence artifact.

schema.name: ApprovalRestsOnSelfAttestation
schema.Description: TRUE when an approval step's verification was either self-witnessed by the approver or never performed at all.

schema.name: ExceptionInvocationCount
schema.Description: How many specified exceptions were invoked during this step execution.

schema.name: RanUnderException
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution invoked at least one specified exception.

schema.name: IsCompleted
schema.Description: TRUE when this step execution reached a completed state.

schema.name: IsVerificationPassed
schema.Description: TRUE only when this step execution's verification actually passed. PENDING and FAIL are both not-passed.

schema.name: IsLegalReviewStep
schema.Description: TRUE for executions of the legal and privacy review step.

schema.name: ClearedLegalReviewKey
schema.Description: Carries the execution id only when this row is a PASSED legal review; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: AssignedRole
schema.Description: The role the specification assigns to this step.

schema.name: RoleCurrentAgent
schema.Description: The agent currently designated to hold the step's assigned role.

schema.name: ExecutorIsDesignatedAgent
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent that executed the step is the agent currently designated for the step's role.

schema.name: InputsWereFreshAtRun
schema.Description: Freshness verdict for the step this execution ran.

schema.name: RanOnStaleInputs
schema.Description: TRUE when an execution completed even though its step's authoritative inputs are stale.

schema.name: UnresolvedIssueCount
schema.Description: Number of unresolved issue occurrences recorded against this execution.

schema.name: HasDeviation
schema.Description: TRUE when a deviation from the specification was recorded.

schema.name: IsClean
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution passed verification, deviated from nothing, left no open issue, and finished on time.

schema.name: ProcedureExecutionWhenUnclean
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step execution is not clean, blank otherwise.

schema.name: EvaluatedRequirementCount
schema.Description: Number of requirements actually scored against this execution.

schema.name: RequiredBlockingCount
schema.Description: Number of blocking requirements the specification demands for this step.

schema.name: HasUnevaluatedBlockingRequirement
schema.Description: TRUE when fewer requirements were scored than the step has blocking requirements.

schema.name: ExecutingAgentKind
schema.Description: The kind of agent that actually performed this step execution.

schema.name: WasExecutedBySoftware
schema.Description: TRUE when software actually performed this step.

schema.name: StepIsSoftwareAssigned
schema.Description: Whether the step being executed was specified as a software step.

schema.name: SoftwareDidHumanWork
schema.Description: TRUE when software performed a step the procedure specified for a human.

schema.name: IsApprovalExecution
schema.Description: Whether this execution is of a designated human approval gate.

schema.name: IsVerified
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution recorded a positive verification outcome.

schema.name: UnconfirmedNonHumanDecisionCount
schema.Description: Number of material non-human decisions in this execution that no human confirmed.

schema.name: RequiresHumanConfirmation
schema.Description: Whether the specification requires human confirmation for this step.

schema.name: HumanConfirmationMissing
schema.Description: TRUE when a step requiring human confirmation contains unconfirmed material non-human decisions.

schema.name: DraftedFromUnusableSource
schema.Description: TRUE when a drafting execution completed despite an unapproved or stale source at its step.

schema.name: InputsWereUsable
schema.Description: Source-usability verdict for the step this execution ran.

schema.name: SoftwareExecutionStepKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: the step this execution ran when it was carried out by software, blank otherwise.

schema.name: StepControlKind
schema.Description: The control kind of the step this execution ran.

schema.name: UnfalsifiedClearanceCount
schema.Description: How many of this step's blocking clearances came from controls that have never once failed.

schema.name: AllClearancesAreUnfalsified
schema.Description: TRUE when every blocking control evaluated on this step is one that has never returned a negative result.

schema.name: StaleAtRunCount
schema.Description: How many authoritative sources this step read that were already out of SLA when it read them.

schema.name: WasStaleWhenIRanIt
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution consumed at least one authoritative source that was stale at the time of reading.

schema.name: StalenessAnswerIsTenseDependent
schema.Description: TRUE when the as-of-now staleness verdict disagrees with the as-of-run verdict for the same step.

schema.name: HasAnyDeclaredCheck
schema.Description: TRUE when the specification declared at least one verification or one blocking requirement for this step.

schema.name: PerformedCheckCount
schema.Description: Total number of checks actually carried out on this execution, verifications plus blocking-control evaluations.

schema.name: DeclaredCheckCount
schema.Description: Total number of checks the specification called for on this step.

schema.name: IsUncheckedByDesign
schema.Description: TRUE when the specification asked for no verification and no blocking control on this step at all.

schema.name: IsVacuouslyClean
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution reads clean and nothing was ever declared that could have made it read otherwise.

schema.name: IsSubstantivelyClean
schema.Description: TRUE when this execution is clean AND every declared check was actually performed.

schema.name: VacuouslyCleanExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step is vacuously clean.

schema.name: UncorroboratedPassCount
schema.Description: How many of this step's passing verifications were observed by the executor with no evidence attached.

schema.name: EvidencePositionIsWeak
schema.Description: TRUE when every verification performed on this step was an uncorroborated self-witnessed pass.

schema.name: PreparationExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step execution is a preparation step.

schema.name: ApprovalExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step execution is an approval step.

schema.name: HasGoverningInstrument
schema.Description: TRUE when this step's departure from spec is covered by an invoked exception or an approved change request against its procedure version.

schema.name: HasApprovedChangeCoverage
schema.Description: Whether the procedure version this step belongs to has an approved change request that could account for a departure.

schema.name: VersionOfStep
schema.Description: The procedure version this step execution's specification step belongs to.

schema.name: IsUngovernedDivergence
schema.Description: TRUE when this step departed from specification and no exception or approved change covers it.

schema.name: UngovernedDivergenceExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this step diverged without governance.

schema.name: SelfAttestedApprovalExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this approval rested on self-attestation.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.Deviation: One source feed was 17 minutes old; controller approved rerun.
Human analyst confirmed all material escalations.

RequirementSatisfactions.Description: Execution-time evaluations of requirements. Maps to pko:RequirementSatisfaction, refersToRequirement, and hasRequirementSatisfactionLevel.

schema.name: RequirementSatisfactionId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one RequirementSatisfactions row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the RequirementSatisfactions row.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: Execution being evaluated.

schema.name: Requirement
schema.Description: Requirement evaluated.

schema.name: SatisfactionLevel
schema.Description: NotEvaluated, NotSatisfied, PartiallySatisfied, or Satisfied.

schema.name: Evidence
schema.Description: Evidence supporting the evaluation.

schema.name: EvaluatedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent that evaluated satisfaction.

schema.name: EvaluatedAt
schema.Description: Evaluation timestamp.

schema.name: RequirementIsBlocking
schema.Description: Whether the requirement being evaluated on this satisfaction row is a blocking control.

schema.name: IsFullySatisfied
schema.Description: TRUE only when the requirement is recorded as fully Satisfied. PartiallySatisfied, Unsatisfied, Waived, and blank are all FALSE.

schema.name: IsBlockingAndUnmet
schema.Description: TRUE when a blocking requirement is recorded at anything less than fully Satisfied. This is the control-failure witness at the requirement grain.

schema.name: BlockingUnmetStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent StepExecution id only when this row is a blocking-unmet violation; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: BlockingSatisfactionStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent StepExecution id when this satisfaction row concerns a blocking requirement.

schema.name: NegativeOutcomeRequirementKey
schema.Description: Echoes the Requirement id only when this evaluation came out at less than fully Satisfied.

schema.name: EvaluatorAgentKind
schema.Description: What kind of agent evaluated this requirement satisfaction.

schema.name: NonHumanEvaluatedHumanControl
schema.Description: TRUE when a blocking requirement was evaluated by a non-human agent.

schema.name: RequirementHasComputedWitness
schema.Description: Whether the requirement this row evaluates has a computed witness behind it.

schema.name: IsAssertedOnly
schema.Description: TRUE when a blocking requirement is recorded as Satisfied purely on human assertion, with no computed predicate behind it.

schema.name: AssertedOnlyExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the grandparent procedure execution id for assertion-only satisfactions.

schema.name: ParentProcedureExecution
schema.Description: The procedure execution this satisfaction ultimately belongs to.

schema.name: StepExecutionWhenScored
schema.Description: Echoes the step-execution id when a satisfaction level was actually recorded, blank otherwise.

schema.name: IsHumanEvaluated
schema.Description: TRUE when a human evaluated this requirement satisfaction.

schema.name: RequirementIsApprovalType
schema.Description: The requirement's type, e.g. Approval, Control, Privacy.

schema.name: IsInvalidApproval
schema.Description: TRUE when an approval-type requirement is not fully satisfied, or was not evaluated by a human.

schema.name: ProcedureExecutionOfSatisfaction
schema.Description: The run this satisfaction belongs to.

schema.name: RunWhenInvalidApproval
schema.Description: Echoes the run id when this is an invalid approval, blank otherwise.

schema.name: RequirementIsUnfalsified
schema.Description: Whether the requirement behind this satisfaction record has never once returned a negative result.

schema.name: IsClearanceByUnfalsifiedControl
schema.Description: TRUE when this record cleared a step against a blocking control that has never produced a negative outcome.

schema.name: UnfalsifiedClearanceStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the step execution id when this clearance came from an unfalsified control.

schema.name: SpecStepOfExecution
schema.Description: The specification step behind the step execution this satisfaction was recorded against.

schema.name: BindingKey
schema.Description: The StepRequirements binding this satisfaction record exercised.

schema.name: ScoredStepExecutorAgent
schema.Description: The agent who executed the step this satisfaction record scores.

schema.name: EvaluatorIsStepExecutor
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent who scored this control is the same agent who performed the step being scored.

schema.name: RunOwnerAgent
schema.Description: The agent accountable for the whole procedure execution this satisfaction sits inside.

schema.name: EvaluatorOwnsTheRun
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent scoring this control is the agent accountable for the execution it belongs to.

schema.name: IsInterestedPartyAssertion
schema.Description: TRUE when a blocking control's only evidence is an assertion made by someone with an interest in the outcome.

schema.name: HasWrittenEvidence
schema.Description: TRUE when this satisfaction record carries any evidence text at all.

schema.name: IsBareAssertion
schema.Description: TRUE when a blocking control was cleared with no computed witness and no written justification.

schema.name: InterestedAssertionExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this record is an interested-party assertion.

schema.name: IsComputedlyWitnessed
schema.Description: TRUE when this record scores a blocking control that has a computed witness behind it.

schema.name: ComputedWitnessExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this control was computationally witnessed.

schema.name: StepExecutorAgent
schema.Description: The agent who executed the step this satisfaction record scores.

schema.name: WasScoredAfterAttestation
schema.Description: TRUE when this control was scored after the run it belongs to had already been attested.

schema.name: AttestationInstantForRun
schema.Description: When the parent execution was attested, if it has been.

schema.name: PostAttestationScoreExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this control was scored after signature.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.Evidence: Preparer and reviewer were different agents.
Draft provenance contains only approved source and elicitation records.
Employment counsel review completed 2026-07-19: notice content, acknowledgement mechanism, and retention period cleared against employment law and TCPA consent requirements.
Reconciliation workpapers were attached to a personal drive, not the 7-year retention archive.
Close package archived to the retention system with a 7-year hold.

Errors.Description: Reusable error definitions encountered during execution. Maps to pko:Error, errorCode, and errorCause.

schema.name: ErrorId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one Errors row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the Errors row.

schema.name: Label
schema.Description: Error label.

schema.name: ErrorCode
schema.Description: Error code; maps to pko:errorCode.

schema.name: ErrorCause
schema.Description: Known or suspected cause; maps to pko:errorCause.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.Label: Source feed timestamp too old
data.ErrorCause: Source integration lag or daylight-saving timestamp normalization issue.

data.Label: SMS provider throttled requests
data.ErrorCause: Provider rate limit or temporary carrier congestion.

IssueOccurrences.Description: Concrete issue events during execution. Maps to pko:IssueOccurrence, hasEncounteredError, wasEncounteredBy, issueCause, and issueSolution.

schema.name: IssueOccurrenceId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one IssueOccurrences row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the IssueOccurrences row.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: Step execution during which the issue occurred.

schema.name: Error
schema.Description: Error encountered.

schema.name: EncounteredByAgent
schema.Description: Agent that encountered or reported the issue.

schema.name: OccurredAt
schema.Description: Occurrence time.

schema.name: IssueCause
schema.Description: Execution-specific cause; maps to pko:issueCause.

schema.name: IssueSolution
schema.Description: Applied solution; maps to pko:issueSolution.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Open, Monitoring, Resolved, or Closed.

schema.name: IsUnresolved
schema.Description: TRUE when this issue occurrence has not been closed out.

schema.name: StepExecutionWhenUnresolved
schema.Description: Echoes the step-execution id when the issue is unresolved, blank otherwise.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.IssueSolution: Reran extraction after source timestamp normalized; added explicit timestamp knowledge fragment.

data.IssueCause: Synthetic channel test observed provider throttling.
data.IssueSolution: Queue with exponential backoff and preserve recipient-local quiet-hours constraint.

UserQuestions.Description: Questions asked by agents during execution. Maps to pko:UserQuestionOccurrence, questionByUser, wasAskedBy, and isQuestionAddressedBy.

schema.name: UserQuestionId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one UserQuestions row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the UserQuestions row.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: Execution context for the question.

schema.name: AskedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent asking the question.

schema.name: AskedAt
schema.Description: Question time.

schema.name: QuestionText
schema.Description: Question text; maps to pko:questionByUser.

schema.name: ResolvedByFaq
schema.Description: FAQ used to resolve the question.

schema.name: AddressedByResource
schema.Description: Resource that addressed the question.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Open, Answered, Escalated, or Closed.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Exact PKO class IRI.

data.QuestionText: Why did the FX variance appear only after the cutover?
Can the drafting AI add a new legal exception if it seems reasonable?

UserFeedback.Description: Feedback supplied by users about a procedure or execution. Maps to pko:UserFeedbackOccurrence, feedbackOnProcedureExecution, and wasProvidedBy.

schema.name: UserFeedbackId

ChangeRequests.Description: Governed requests for semantic or operational change, anchored to a procedure version and authority. Explicit ERB-PKO extension.

schema.name: ChangeRequestId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one ChangeRequests row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the ChangeRequests row.

schema.name: ProcedureVersion
schema.Description: Procedure version affected.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Change title.

schema.name: ChangeKind
schema.Description: Defect, Enhancement, NewRequirement, DataOperation, or BreakingChange.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Draft, UnderReview, Approved, Rejected, Implemented, or Closed.

schema.name: RequestedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent requesting the change.

schema.name: AuthorityRole
schema.Description: Role authorized to approve the change.

schema.name: RequestedAt
schema.Description: Request time.

schema.name: DecidedAt
schema.Description: Decision time.

schema.name: ImpactAssessment
schema.Description: Expected effect on commitments, data, projections, and tests.

schema.name: IsOpen
schema.Description: TRUE while the change request is still outstanding. An Approved request stays open until ImplementedAt records that it actually landed — approval is a decision, not an outcome.

schema.name: OpenChangeVersionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the ProcedureVersion id only for change requests still open.

schema.name: IsDecided
schema.Description: TRUE when a decision timestamp has been recorded.

schema.name: EvaluationContext
schema.Description: The evaluation context this row's time-dependent witnesses are judged under.

schema.name: AsOfInstant
schema.Description: The evaluation instant this row's time-dependent witnesses are judged against.

schema.name: DaysPending
schema.Description: Days from request to decision, or to now if still undecided.

schema.name: IsStillPending
schema.Description: TRUE when the request is in an open status and has no decision recorded.

schema.name: IsStalled
schema.Description: TRUE when an undecided change request has been pending more than fourteen days.

schema.name: AuthorityAgent
schema.Description: The agent currently holding the authority role that owes a decision on this request.

schema.name: RequesterIsAuthority
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent who raised the request is also the agent who decides it.

schema.name: AwaitsAuthorityDecision
schema.Description: TRUE when this request is formally before its authority and no decision has been recorded.

schema.name: AuthorityRoleLabel
schema.Description: Human-readable label of the role that owes a decision.

schema.name: TouchesLiveVersion
schema.Description: Whether the version this request would alter is currently executable.

schema.name: IsLiveDecisionBacklog
schema.Description: TRUE when a decision I owe is blocking a change to a procedure currently in production.

schema.name: BlocksAnOpenGap
schema.Description: TRUE when an undecided request against a live version is the kind that exists to close a known gap.

schema.name: ImplementedAt
schema.Description: When the approved change was actually applied to the procedure version. Null until it lands. Distinct from DecidedAt, which records only that the authority ruled. Approval and implementation are separate events; conflating them is what left IsOpen with no terminal state.

schema.name: BacklogVersionKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: this request's procedure version when it is live decision backlog, blank otherwise.

schema.name: IsMyPendingDecision
schema.Description: A change request awaiting a decision that is mine personally to make.

schema.name: IsMyBlockingBacklog
schema.Description: A decision waiting on me that is holding an open gap on a live procedure.

schema.name: IsMyOverdueBacklog
schema.Description: A blocking decision that has waited on me for more than two weeks.

schema.name: IsImplemented
schema.Description: Whether the approved change has actually been applied.

schema.name: IsMyDecidedRequest
schema.Description: A change request I have personally ruled on.

schema.name: IsMyDecidedButUnlanded
schema.Description: A decision I have made that has not yet been applied — still counted against me by the loop-1 open-request measure.

schema.name: DecisionLatencyDays
schema.Description: How many days I took to rule, once ruled. Zero when undecided — read only alongside IsDecided.

schema.name: ImplementationLatencyDays
schema.Description: How many days elapsed between my ruling and the change actually landing. Zero when not yet implemented.

schema.name: DelayIsDownstreamOfMe
schema.Description: A request I decided promptly that is nevertheless still outstanding because nobody has implemented it.

schema.name: UnlandedVersionKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: this request's procedure version when I have decided it but it has not landed, blank otherwise.

schema.name: IsApprovedNotImplemented
schema.Description: A change request the authority approved but that has not yet been applied.

schema.name: DaysSinceApproval
schema.Description: How many days have elapsed since the authority decided this request. Zero when undecided.

schema.name: IsStalledImplementation
schema.Description: An approved change request that has sat unimplemented for more than two weeks.

schema.name: StalledImplementationVersionKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: this request's procedure version when its implementation is stalled, blank otherwise.

schema.name: ApprovedVersionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the target version id when this change request was approved.

schema.name: IsApprovedDecision
schema.Description: TRUE when this change request was decided in the affirmative.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri

data.Title: The founding questions
data.Premise: The model specified obligations precisely and witnessed their breach almost nowhere. Requirements, verifications, exceptions, and communication policies were all stated with real precision, and in each case the execution-side counterpart that would catch a violation was missing. Loop 1 asks each of the twelve named roles what it actually needs to know, and invents the atomic predicates required to answer.

data.Title: Questions only loop 1 made askable
data.Premise: Loop 1 established WHAT can go wrong: a blocking control unmet, a verification skipped, a send refused, a claim whose source has left. Loop 2 asks the questions that only exist once those columns do — about the QUALITY of the assurance rather than its presence. Three examples that had no expressible form before loop 1 ran: only 4 of 13 requirements have any computed witness at all, so which controls are still trusted purely on assertion; verification_outcomes.is_self_witnessed reads 3/4, so how much of the evidence is the executor vouching for themselves; and send_intents.is_silently_dropped reads 4/7, a failure mode that could not be named until IsClearedToSend existed to refuse a send in the first place. Every loop-2 question records, in its own provenance, the loop-1 predicates that made it possible.

RoleQuestions.Description: One row per question a named role wants answered. Every invented predicate in this rulebook traces back to one of these.

schema.name: RoleQuestionId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one RoleQuestions row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the RoleQuestions row.

schema.name: AskingRole
schema.Description: The role that wants this question answered.

schema.name: WitnessLoop
schema.Description: The expansion loop in which this question was posed.

schema.name: QuestionText
schema.Description: The question in the role's own words.

schema.name: WhyItMatters
schema.Description: What goes wrong in the real world when this question cannot be answered.

schema.name: AnswerableBefore
schema.Description: TRUE if the model could already answer this before its loop ran. FALSE means the loop had to invent predicates for it.

schema.name: PredicateCount
schema.Description: How many fields were invented to answer this question.

schema.name: IsAnswered
schema.Description: TRUE when at least one predicate exists to answer this question.

schema.name: WitnessedAnswer
schema.Description: The current reading of this question's predicates, extracted from the substrate after the loop ran: which witness columns fire, on how many rows out of how many. Written by tools/extract_computed_answers.py from values Postgres computed — never recomputed in Python. This is what lets a later loop plan against materialized answers instead of imagined ones.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI.

data.QuestionText: On the steps I personally executed, did any of them close out while a BLOCKING requirement was still not fully satisfied?
data.WhyItMatters: I executed se-close03 (Reconcile material accounts), which carries TWO blocking requirements via sr-c03a (req-close-balance) and sr-c03b (req-close-evidence). Today the model records SatisfactionLevel as free text and nothing compares it against IsBlocking. If I close a step with a PartiallySatisfied blocking control — exactly the shape sat-policy-legal is in right now — the package moves on and nobody is told. I want that to be a computed red flag on my own row before it becomes an audit finding on my name.

data.QuestionText: Which blocking requirements were attached to a step I ran but never got a satisfaction record at all — not satisfied, not unsatisfied, just never assessed?
data.WhyItMatters: This is the failure mode that scares me more than a recorded 'Unsatisfied'. sr-c03b binds req-close-evidence to close-03. I ran close-03 as se-close03. There is no RequirementSatisfactions row for that pairing. The model shows green because absence reads as silence, not as a gap. An unevaluated blocking control is indistinguishable from a passed one, and that is the exact thing SOX testing is designed to catch.

data.QuestionText: When I attested my reconciliation, was the underlying ledger data actually fresh — or was I certifying against a source that had already gone stale under its own SLA?
data.WhyItMatters: OperationalBindings.IsFresh already exists and works, but it is evaluated against NOW() and lives on a table nothing in the execution story reads. My step execution has no idea whether the resources it depended on were inside their freshness SLA. se-close02 already recorded a 17-minute feed against a 15-minute SLA and it took a human writing prose into a Deviation field to surface it. I want that connected structurally, because next time the prose might not get written.

data.QuestionText: Which of my steps ran long without anybody recording why — and of those, which ones had an exception available that nobody invoked?
data.WhyItMatters: StepExecutions.IsLate already works. But 'late' on its own is not a finding; late-and-unexplained is. se-close03 is mine and it ran nearly 19 hours. If that overran the expected duration and the Deviation field is empty, I have an undocumented overrun sitting on my name. And exc-ledger-outage exists precisely for the close-02 condition — if it was never invoked, the model should say that out loud instead of letting a blank Deviation stand in for 'nothing happened'.

data.QuestionText: For every step I ran that had a declared verification, did anybody actually perform it and record the observed signal — or did I just get a PASS with nothing behind it?
data.WhyItMatters: StepVerifications is entirely spec-side. verify-reconciliation declares that close-03 must show unexplained-material-variance = 0. I ran close-03. My StepExecution says VerificationResult=PASS. But there is no row anywhere recording what the signal ACTUALLY read. The PASS is an unbacked assertion typed into a field. If an auditor asks me to produce the observed value, the model has nothing — and a skipped verification looks identical to a passed one.

data.QuestionText: Did the same agent both prepare and approve any part of this close? req-close-separation says the preparer may not be the final approver — show me that it actually held, on exec-close-2026-q2, computed and not asserted.
data.WhyItMatters: This is the single highest-value gap in the model and it is my control. req-close-separation is IsBlocking=true, bound to close-05 via sr-c05, and sat-close-separation records SatisfactionLevel='Satisfied' with the evidence string 'Preparer and reviewer were different agents.' That string is a human typing a conclusion into a text field. NOTHING compares se-close03.ExecutedByAgent (maria-chen) against se-close05.ExecutedByAgent (devon-okafor) or se-close06.ExecutedByAgent (priya-raman). Segregation of duties is the control SOX cares about most and in this model it is prose. If maria-chen executed both the preparation and the approval tomorrow, sat-close-separation would still say 'Satisfied' and the model would agree.

data.QuestionText: Every approval on this close — was it given by an agent who actually held the required role AT THE TIME they approved, not just someone who holds it today?
data.WhyItMatters: req-close-human-approval is blocking and says controller and CFO approvals must be human-confirmed. sat-close-human records 'Satisfied' because a human name appears in a field. But nothing checks that the approving agent held the approving role. RoleAssignments proves this is not paranoia: ra-old-variance shows maria-chen held variance-review-agent until 2026-03-31 and ra-new-variance shows variance-ai took it on 2026-04-01. Role-holding is time-bounded and it genuinely changes. If someone approves close-06 who is not the CFO, or who was the CFO last quarter but not this one, I need that computed. And exc-cfo-unavailable explicitly permits delegation to an Acting CFO 'never to the preparer' — so an authority check that cannot see delegation is incomplete.

data.QuestionText: Which of my blocking close controls are structurally incapable of failing — declared, bound to a step, and yet with no mechanism that could ever produce a negative result?
data.WhyItMatters: req-close-cutoff, req-close-balance, req-close-separation, and req-close-evidence are all IsBlocking=true. All four are pure prose. A control that cannot fail is not a control, it is a comment. Before I sign anything I want a list of my own controls ranked by whether they can actually operate. This is the meta-question — it is about the model's ability to hold me accountable, and it is the one I would ask first if I were the external auditor rather than the controller.

data.QuestionText: Give me one number per close: did exec-close-2026-q2 actually conform to close-v1.1.0, and if not, how far off was it?
data.WhyItMatters: ProcedureExecutions has ZERO derived fields. The parent of the entire execution story makes no claim whatsoever — it has a status someone typed and two timestamps. Every failure signal in this model lives on child rows, so to know whether a close was clean I have to read twelve StepExecutions and six RequirementSatisfactions by hand. That is not a control environment, that is a filing cabinet. I want conformance to be a computed property of the execution, so that 'the close was clean' is a claim the model makes and can be wrong about.

data.QuestionText: Did any AI agent or automated pipeline execute a step that requires human confirmation, or sign off on something a human was supposed to own?
data.WhyItMatters: req-close-human-approval and req-policy-human-approval both say a human must approve. Steps carries RequiresHumanConfirmation as a raw boolean and Agents carries AgentKind — Human, AIAgent, AutomatedPipeline — and NOTHING compares them. variance-ai executed se-close04. policy-drafting-ai executed se-policy03. Both fine today because those steps are not approval steps. But ra-old-variance to ra-new-variance shows this org actively migrates work from humans to AI agents, and the control that is supposed to stop that migration reaching an approval step is prose. This is the control I expect to matter most over the next two years and it currently does not exist.

data.QuestionText: Can I sign the attestation for exec-close-2026-q2 — yes or no — and if no, name the blocker.
data.WhyItMatters: This is the only question I actually have. Today the model answers it with ProcedureExecutions.ExecutionStatus='Completed', which is a string somebody typed into a field. My signature carries personal liability under Section 302. I need attestation-readiness to be a computed conjunction of every blocking control, such that if ANY control did not operate, the model says no and names it. A green status that cannot go red is worse than no status at all, because it manufactures false comfort at exactly the moment I am least able to verify it myself.

data.QuestionText: Am I being asked to approve an execution of a procedure version that is itself overdue for review, or that has an open change request against it?
data.WhyItMatters: I sign against close-v1.1.0. If that version's periodic review lapsed, or if there is an open ChangeRequest saying the procedure is known to be wrong, then I am attesting to conformance with a specification that the organization itself no longer stands behind. ReviewEvents.IsOverdue already works and ChangeRequests.IsOpen already works, but neither is connected to the execution I am signing. The version's fitness and the execution's conformance are two different questions and I need both answered before I sign, not one.

data.QuestionText: My own approval on se-close06 — is there anything behind it other than the fact that I clicked approve?
data.WhyItMatters: verify-cfo declares that close-06 must show cfo-approval = APPROVED. se-close06 says VerificationResult=PASS. But the PASS and the approval are the same act: I approved, therefore the verification that I approved passes. That is circular, and it is my name on the circle. sat-close-human's evidence reads 'Human CFO approval recorded' — recorded by whom, against what? I want to know whether my approval rests on independently verified upstream evidence or whether the model is simply reflecting my own click back at me.

data.QuestionText: For every approval in this close, tell me the human who was personally accountable — and whether an exception was used to route authority away from the person who normally holds it.
data.WhyItMatters: exc-cfo-unavailable exists and says approval may be delegated to a designated Acting CFO, 'never to the preparer'. That last clause is a control and it is prose inside a text field. If I was unavailable and someone approved in my place, I need the model to show me who, under what authority, and whether the never-to-the-preparer condition held. Right now an exception can be invoked with no trace at all — nothing links an execution back to the exception it used. From my seat that means delegated authority is entirely invisible, which is the exposure I am least willing to carry.

data.QuestionText: Across everything I am signing, is there any control claim in this model that rests on a human typing a conclusion rather than on something computed?
data.WhyItMatters: This is the question behind all my other questions. sat-close-separation says 'Preparer and reviewer were different agents.' sat-close-human says 'Human CFO approval recorded.' Both are prose conclusions in an Evidence field, both assert that a control operated, and neither is checkable. I want the model to distinguish between a control that was COMPUTED to have held and one that was ASSERTED to have held, and to tell me how much of my attestation rests on the second kind. If most of my assurance is assertion, I want to know that before I sign, not after.

data.QuestionText: For close-v1.1.0 I promised a 90-day review cadence and for policy-v1.0.0 a 60-day cadence. Is the ACTUAL interval since the last review already longer than the cadence I promised?
data.WhyItMatters: ReviewCadenceDays is a promise. ReviewEvents.ReviewedAt is the behavior. Today the model stores both and compares neither, so a stewardship assignment can sit in permanent silent breach while ReviewEvents.IsOverdue stays FALSE because NextReviewDue was hand-set to a date that has nothing to do with the promised cadence. I need the promise and the behavior in the same row.

data.QuestionText: Which procedure versions have no steward at all? close-v1.0.0 is Archived but still referenced by ProcedureVersionLinks — does anyone own it?
data.WhyItMatters: An unstewarded version is nobody's problem until it is everybody's problem. There are 3 ProcedureVersions and only 2 StewardshipAssignments; the model has never once been asked to notice the difference. Orphaned versions accumulate silently as new versions ship and the old assignment is never extended or retired.

data.QuestionText: close-04-fallback-close-03 and policy-07-fallback-policy-06 are the paths we take when things go wrong. Have they EVER been walked in a real execution?
data.WhyItMatters: There are 19 StepTransitions and only 12 StepExecutions. Every Fallback and Alternative transition in this model is untested. Untested fallbacks are exactly where an outage becomes an incident — the day the ERP is down is the wrong day to discover the recovery path was never real. Nothing in the model witnesses this.
data.WitnessedAnswer: StepExecutions.IsCompleted=12/12; StepTransitions.HasBeenTraversed=10/19; StepTransitions.HasReachableOrigin=14/19; StepTransitions.HasReachableTarget=13/19; StepTransitions.IsNeverExercised=6/19; StepTransitions.IsRecoveryPath=4/19; StepTransitions.IsUntestedRecoveryPath=1/19; StepTransitions.IsUnwalkedRecoveryPath=4/19

data.QuestionText: Is any procedure version live with an Open BLOCKING knowledge gap against it?
data.WhyItMatters: gap-close-dual-outage was Blocking and is now Resolved — good. But nothing stops the next one from staying Open while the version keeps shipping. ProcedureVersions.CountOfOpenKnowledgeGaps exists but is blind to BlockingKind, so a Blocking gap and a cosmetic one count the same. IsReadyForExecution then fails on trivia and stays silent on the thing that should stop the line.

data.QuestionText: cr-policy-delivery was requested on 2026-07-19 and has no DecidedAt. How long has it been sitting, and is that longer than we tolerate?
data.WhyItMatters: A change request with no decision is a decision — to do nothing — but it never announces itself. The model stores RequestedAt and DecidedAt and computes neither the interval nor a staleness verdict. Since cr-policy-delivery is the vehicle for closing gap-policy-delivery-receipts on a live policy version, its stall is load-bearing.

data.QuestionText: kf-close-fx-time, kf-close-top-ten and kf-close-judgment all trace to devon-okafor via elicit-close-shadow. If Devon's controller role assignment ends, which of our beliefs lose their witness?
data.WhyItMatters: This is institutional memory decay in its purest form. A knowledge fragment is a claim; its SourceAgent is the person who can defend it. When that person's RoleAssignment.ValidTo passes, the claim survives in the database but its warrant evaporates — nobody left can say why we believe it. The chain KnowledgeFragments -> SourceAgent -> RoleAssignments.ValidTo is fully modeled and never traversed. Three of seven fragments hang off one human.

data.QuestionText: kf-policy-manager-route is Status=Reviewed, not Approved, so IsCurrentlyValid is FALSE. policy-08 is a live step. Are we running a procedure on knowledge I never approved?
data.WhyItMatters: This is the exact failure the separation of Reviewed and Approved exists to catch, and nothing catches it. The fragment describes the 48-hour manager-routing behavior that exc-unreachable depends on. It is attached to a step in an Approved procedure version, it is being relied upon operationally, and it has never passed my desk. The model knows every piece of this and joins none of it.

data.QuestionText: elicit-close-shadow ran on 2026-04-03 and elicit-policy-workshop on 2026-05-10. How old is the evidence behind each belief, and at what age do I stop trusting a shadowing session I have not repeated?
data.WhyItMatters: Knowledge does not announce its own expiry. Every fragment here has ValidTo=null, which reads as 'valid forever' — but a Tacit claim captured by shadowing one controller for three hours in April is a snapshot of a practice that drifts. I want the age of the EVIDENCE, not the age of the record, and I want it graded by how fragile the elicitation method was.

data.QuestionText: What do we know that we do not know? gap-policy-delivery-receipts has been Open since 2026-07-18 with a ResolutionPlan and no owner activity. How long do our acknowledged ignorances stay acknowledged and unresolved?
data.WhyItMatters: A KnowledgeGap is the most honest object in this model: a written admission of what we do not know. But an admission with no clock on it converts into a permanent condition. KnowledgeGaps has IdentifiedAt and a Status and computes no age, no ownership currency, and no relationship between a gap's severity and how long we have tolerated it. Known unknowns that never age are not being managed, they are being filed.

data.QuestionText: cr-close-timestamp altered a blocking control on close-v1.1.0 and was decided by me. Did any semantic change to a live procedure reach production without a change request and without a review event behind it?
data.WhyItMatters: My authority is over what counts as true, which means every change to the meaning of a live procedure must trace to a decision I made. close-v1.1.0 was ModifiedAt 2026-07-02T15:00 by maria-chen; review-close-q2 happened at 16:00 and cr-close-timestamp was raised at 16:30. That ordering is fine. But nothing in the model checks the ordering, so an edit that PRECEDES no review and no change request looks identical to a governed one. Provenance of change is as much an epistemic question as provenance of belief.

data.QuestionText: Did we send an SMS to any recipient who had not given SMS consent at the moment we sent it?
data.WhyItMatters: comm-sms-policy sets ConsentRequired=true. Every SMS to a non-consenting recipient is an independent statutory violation with per-message damages, and the exposure scales with the size of the affected population. Right now the model cannot answer this at all -- there are no per-recipient send records -- so counsel cannot sign off on policy-04, cannot certify to an outside regulator, and cannot even scope the exposure. The absence of the record IS the legal problem: unanswerable and non-compliant look identical from the outside.

data.QuestionText: Did any SMS go out between 20:00 and 08:00 in the recipient's own local time?
data.WhyItMatters: comm-sms-policy declares QuietHoursStart 20:00 / QuietHoursEnd 08:00. Quiet-hours restrictions on non-emergency messaging are statutory in several jurisdictions and contractual in others, and a night-time text to an employee about an employment policy change is exactly the fact pattern that generates a complaint. The window wraps midnight, which is where naive implementations quietly get it wrong -- and a witness that is wrong in the safe direction is worse than no witness, because it manufactures false assurance.

data.QuestionText: For every recipient we could not reach, did we correctly refrain from marking their acknowledgement complete?
data.WhyItMatters: exc-unreachable is explicit: create an HR case, route to the manager, and do NOT mark acknowledgement complete. A falsely-recorded acknowledgement is the single most dangerous artifact in this entire model -- it is an affirmative written misrepresentation that the employee received and accepted a policy change, and it is exactly the document that gets subpoenaed in a wrongful-termination or unpaid-notice claim. Deemed-acknowledgement by silence is not acknowledgement. I would rather have zero acknowledgements than one fabricated one.

data.QuestionText: Do we still hold the exact text of every message we sent, for the full 2555-day retention period the policy commits us to?
data.WhyItMatters: Both comm-sms-policy and comm-email-policy declare RetentionDays=2555 -- seven years. That is a commitment we made in writing, and in a dispute the question is never 'what did the template say', it is 'what did THIS employee actually receive'. Templates contain variable substitutions; the rendered text is the evidence. If a delivery record exists but its rendered body is empty, we have a receipt with no document behind it, and the adverse inference runs against us.

data.QuestionText: Did any message go out before my legal review of the underlying policy version was actually complete?
data.WhyItMatters: policy-04 is my step, and right now se-policy04 is InProgress with VerificationResult PENDING. If policy-07 sends started while my review was still open, then employees received a policy communication that no lawyer had cleared -- and the model would show that in black and white. This is not process pedantry: an unreviewed employment-policy communication can create a binding representation to the workforce, and once it is sent we cannot un-send it. The ordering constraint is the control.

data.QuestionText: Did any SMS we sent exceed 160 characters or split into more than 3 segments?
data.WhyItMatters: comm-sms-policy sets MaxMessageLength=160 and MaxSegments=3, and I own that policy. An over-length SMS silently splits at the carrier, arrives out of order, and -- this is the part that actually hurts -- the opt-out language is in the LAST segment, so an over-length message can strip the STOP instruction off the end of what people actually read. I have the template body and I have the policy limit sitting in the same rulebook and nothing compares them. This is the most embarrassing category of failure because it was computable from data we already had.

data.QuestionText: Was every template we actually sent approved by me -- the ApprovalRole the policy names -- before it went out?
data.WhyItMatters: Both channel policies name communications-manager as ApprovalRole. Right now tmpl-policy-sms.Status says 'Approved' and there is not one byte of data anywhere saying WHO approved it or WHEN. A status string with no approver behind it is not an approval, it is a claim. I need to be able to point at a row and say 'that is my name, that is the timestamp, that is the version of the text I saw'. Otherwise when something goes out wrong, 'approved' means nothing and the accountability lands wherever it lands.

data.QuestionText: Did every SMS we actually transmitted carry the approved opt-out text?
data.WhyItMatters: comm-sms-policy RequiredContent says 'Short notice, secure link, approved opt-out text' and the approved template ends with 'Reply STOP to opt out of SMS; email notices continue.' The opt-out is not decoration -- it is the mechanism by which consent stays meaningful, and it is the thing that gets clipped first when a message runs long. I want to check the RENDERED body, not the template, because that is what the employee saw. A policy that declares RequiredContent and never verifies it is a policy that documents an intention.

data.QuestionText: For this policy run, what fraction of the affected population actually received something -- and how many are sitting in a bounced or failed state that nobody is working?
data.WhyItMatters: This is the question I get asked in the Monday stand-up and I currently answer it from a spreadsheet somebody built by hand. It is also the operational face of my own open item gap-policy-delivery-receipts and the change request I already filed, cr-policy-delivery. A bounce that nobody triages is functionally identical to never having sent the message, except that it produces a record that makes it LOOK like we did. That is the failure mode I most want visible.

data.QuestionText: Has any template been edited since I approved it, and did we send the edited version under cover of my old approval?
data.WhyItMatters: This is the failure I have actually lived through. A template gets approved, then somebody makes 'a tiny wording fix' to the body, and the Status field still cheerfully says Approved because nothing ever reset it. The approval attaches to a version of the text, not to a row id. If the body changed and the approval did not, then my name is on something I never read -- and given that these are employment-policy communications, that is not a governance abstraction, that is my name on a legal notice I did not review.

data.QuestionText: Before I transmit, am I permitted to use this channel for this recipient at all?
data.WhyItMatters: I am the component that actually calls the carrier. Every other question in this rulebook is asked after I have already acted, which means every other question is asked too late. I need one column I can read that says PERMITTED or NOT PERMITTED, computed from the policy rows rather than from anything hardcoded in my own configuration -- because my configuration is not the source of truth and should not be. If I cannot determine consent, the answer must be no. An unevaluable rule is a refusal, not a pass.
data.WitnessedAnswer: CommunicationPolicies.IsActivePolicy=2/2; SendIntents.ConsentGatePassed=6/7; SendIntents.IntentRequiresConsent=6/7; SendIntents.PermissionGatePassed=6/7; SendIntents.PolicyIsActive=7/7; SendIntents.ReachabilityGatePassed=6/7; SendIntents.RecipientHasChannelConsent=6/7; SendIntents.RecipientIsEmailReachable=7/7; SendIntents.RecipientIsSmsReachable=6/7

data.QuestionText: Before I transmit, is the current moment inside a window this channel forbids?
data.WhyItMatters: Timing is the one gate I can get right unilaterally and the one that is easiest to get wrong silently. My scheduler runs continuously; nothing about being invoked at 22:40 tells me I should not act. The quiet-hours window is recipient-local and it wraps midnight, so 06:15 is forbidden even though six is less than twenty. I need this evaluated as data on the intent row rather than as arithmetic inside my own code, because arithmetic inside my own code is exactly what nobody can audit and exactly what drifted from the policy in the first place.

data.QuestionText: Before I transmit, is the exact text I am holding within this channel's content rules?
data.WhyItMatters: I render the body; therefore I am the last component that can measure it before it becomes a fact. The template can be compliant and my rendered output still not be, because I am the one substituting the URL and the date. Measuring my own output against the policy's MaxMessageLength, MaxSegments and required opt-out phrase costs me nothing and is the only point in the whole procedure where the comparison is both possible and still preventable. Doing it afterward, as the retrospective witnesses do, tells someone what I did wrong. Doing it here stops it.

data.QuestionText: Before I transmit, has every human authorization my instructions depend on actually been granted?
data.WhyItMatters: policy-07 tells me to enforce channel rules and send APPROVED messages. I am not competent to judge whether a message should exist -- that is the communications manager's approval and the employment counsel's review. What I am competent to do is refuse to act until both are recorded as granted. Today I have no such column, which means I have been treating the absence of an objection as an approval. That is the inverted default and it is how an AI drafting agent's self-approval ended up on a live send. I want the gate to require positive evidence of authorization, so that missing data blocks me instead of releasing me.
