_meta._CMCC_Summary: PKO 2.0.0 expressed as a versioned Effortless Rulebook with specification/execution separation, semantic mappings, governance, knowledge elicitation, live data bindings, and deterministic projections.

__meta__.Description: Project-level metadata that travels with the rulebook: tagline, motif, narrative descriptions, PKO version contract, substrate list, signature rows, etc. One row per metadata key. Use ValueType to interpret StringValue vs JsonValue.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: The metadata key (e.g. 'tagline', 'motif_palette', 'substrates'). Unique within the table.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Identifier for this metadata entry. Mirrors MetaKey so the row is addressable by Name like every other table.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: How to interpret the value columns: 'string' (use StringValue), 'object' (parse JsonValue as JSON object), 'array' (parse JsonValue as JSON array).

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Plain string value. Populated when ValueType == 'string'; null otherwise.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value. Populated when ValueType == 'object' or 'array'; null when ValueType == 'string'.

data.Name: tagline
data.StringValue: One canonical PKO-native rulebook; every procedure document is a projection.

data.Name: description_rich
data.StringValue: A single Effortless Rulebook whose structural model is aligned to the Procedural Knowledge Ontology (PKO) 2.0.0. It keeps procedure *specifications* separate from procedure *executions*, and carries versions, lifecycle status changes, steps, transitions, alternatives and fallbacks, actions, software functions, tools, requirements and verifications, agents, roles and time-bounded role assignments, resources and provenance, errors, issues, questions, feedback, explanations, tacit/implicit/explicit/situated knowledge, elicitation provenance, knowledge gaps, stewardship, change requests, reviews, communities of practice, and communication policy. Natural-language docs, financial SOPs, email/SMS policies, and a governance handbook are all projections of this one model — never hand-maintained documents.

data.Name: use_cases
data.JsonValue: ["Publish an SOP and its governance record from one source instead of two drifting documents.", "Separate what a procedure *says* from what an execution actually *did*, and audit the gap.", "Carry tacit know-how, elicitation provenance, and known knowledge gaps as first-class data.", "Adapt a foreign process format into PKO once, and inherit every downstream projection."]

data.Name: signature_rows
data.JsonValue: [{"table": "Procedures", "why": "Specification of a procedure — distinct from any execution of it."}, {"table": "ProcedureExecutions", "why": "What actually happened, linked back to the spec via pko:hasExecutedProcedure."}, {"table": "StepTransitions", "why": "Default, alternative, and fallback edges — the control flow as data."}, {"table": "KnowledgeGaps", "why": "Known unknowns tracked with severity, ownership, and blocking status."}, {"table": "SemanticMappings", "why": "Every table's exact PKO term, or its explicit extension IRI."}]

data.Name: procedure_families
data.JsonValue: ["Quarter-End Financial Close", "Workforce Policy Change and Employee Notification"]

data.Name: journal_seed
data.StringValue: Started from a standalone PKO prototype and onboarded it as a first-class domain: the rulebook is the source of truth, and the prose is downstream.

data.Name: attribution
data.StringValue: PKO was created by Valentina Anita Carriero, Mario Scrocca, Ilaria Baroni, Antonia Azzini, and Irene Celino, and is published under CC BY 4.0. This domain aligns to PKO; it is not an official PKO distribution and implies no endorsement. See NOTICE.md.

data.Name: CMCC_Summary
data.StringValue: Procedural knowledge is modeled as data, not prose. PKO is graph-shaped; an Effortless Rulebook is an acyclic structural graph, so repeated and many-to-many semantics are promoted to first-class junction and event entities (StepTransitions, StepRequirements, RoleAssignments). No semantics are lost — the relational shape is a faithful encoding, and each projection recovers its own native surface form from the same structure.

schema.name: ExceptionInvocationId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one ExceptionInvocations row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: The execution during which the exception was invoked.

schema.name: Exception
schema.Description: The specified exception that was invoked.

schema.name: InvokedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent who invoked the exception.

schema.name: ApprovedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent who approved the invocation. Must satisfy the exception's ApprovalRole.

schema.name: InvokedAt
schema.Description: When the exception was invoked.

schema.name: HandlingApplied
schema.Description: What was actually done, in the invoker's words.

schema.name: ExpectedHandling
schema.Description: The handling the specification prescribes.

schema.name: RequiredApprovalRole
schema.Description: The role the specification requires to approve this exception.

schema.name: RequiredApprovalRoleHolder
schema.Description: The agent who currently holds the role that must approve this exception.

schema.name: ApprovalRoleMatches
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent who approved is the holder of the role the exception requires.

schema.name: IsApproved
schema.Description: TRUE when an approver was recorded at all.

schema.name: IsImproperlyApproved
schema.Description: TRUE when an exception was invoked without an approver, or approved by an agent who does not hold the required role.

schema.name: InvokerAgentKind
schema.Description: What kind of agent invoked the exception.

schema.name: InvokerAlsoPreparedKey
schema.Description: Composite execution+approver key, in the same key space as StepExecutions.PreparerAgentKey.

schema.name: ParentProcedureExecution
schema.Description: The procedure execution this invocation belongs to.

schema.name: ApproverPreparedCount
schema.Description: How many preparation steps in the same execution were run by the agent who approved this exception.

schema.name: DelegatedToPreparer
schema.Description: TRUE when an exception routed approval authority to an agent who prepared work in the same execution.

schema.name: IsUngovernedInvocation
schema.Description: TRUE when an exception was invoked without proper role authority, or routed authority to the preparer.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension IRI — PKO does not define exception invocation.

data.HandlingApplied: Used the digitally signed 09:07 trial-balance snapshot and opened blocking incident issue-close-feed. No spreadsheet substitution.

schema.name: VerificationOutcomeId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one VerificationOutcomes row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: The execution during which the verification was performed.

schema.name: StepVerification
schema.Description: The declared verification being performed.

schema.name: ObservedSignalValue
schema.Description: The value the signal actually read at verification time.

schema.name: ObservedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent who observed the signal.

schema.name: ObservedAt
schema.Description: When the signal was observed.

schema.name: EvidenceUri
schema.Description: Pointer to the retained artifact backing the observation.

schema.name: ExpectedSignalValue
schema.Description: The value the specification expects.

schema.name: SignalIdentifier
schema.Description: Which signal this outcome concerns.

schema.name: SignalMatchesExpected
schema.Description: TRUE when the observed value equals the expected value.

schema.name: HasEvidence
schema.Description: TRUE when a retained artifact backs this observation.

schema.name: IsUnbackedObservation
schema.Description: TRUE when a verification was recorded as matching but no evidence artifact was retained.

schema.name: IsSelfWitnessed
schema.Description: TRUE when the agent who observed the verification signal is the same agent who executed the step being verified.

schema.name: StepExecutorAgent
schema.Description: The agent who executed the step this verification outcome concerns.

schema.name: SelfWitnessedStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the StepExecution id only for self-witnessed verifications.

schema.name: UnbackedStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the StepExecution id when a verification matched but retained no evidence.

schema.name: IsSelfWitnessedAndUnbacked
schema.Description: TRUE when the executor observed their own passing signal and attached no evidence.

schema.name: IsUncorroboratedPass
schema.Description: TRUE when a PASSING signal was self-observed with no evidence behind it.

schema.name: UncorroboratedPassStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the step execution id when this outcome is an uncorroborated pass.

schema.name: ObserverIsNonHuman
schema.Description: Whether the agent who recorded this observation is non-human. Agents has no PrimaryRole column and an FK from Agents back to Roles would create a table cycle, so the independence question is answered by agent kind rather than by role.

schema.name: ObserverIsIndependentOfExecutor
schema.Description: TRUE when someone other than the step's executor recorded the observation.

schema.name: IsIndependentHumanObservation
schema.Description: TRUE when a human other than the step's executor observed this signal and attached evidence.

schema.name: IndependentObservationExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution id when this is an independent human observation.

schema.name: ParentProcedureExecutionOfOutcome
schema.Description: The procedure execution this verification outcome belongs to.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension IRI — PKO 2.0.0 has no execution-side verification outcome class.

ObservedTransitions.Description: The proxy above cannot tell a walked fallback from a happy-path step that happens to share an endpoint. PKO models Transition as a first-class thing; its execution counterpart is missing. Without a table that records WHICH transition a step execution arrived by, 'has this fallback ever been walked' is permanently unanswerable rather than merely unanswered. This is an extension (urn:effortless:pko-extension#ObservedTransition), not a native PKO term.

schema.name: ObservedTransitionId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one ObservedTransitions row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias.

schema.name: ProcedureExecution
schema.Description: The procedure execution during which this transition was traversed.

schema.name: StepTransition
schema.Description: The specification-level transition that was actually taken.

schema.name: ArrivingStepExecution
schema.Description: The step execution that this traversal produced.

schema.name: ObservedAt
schema.Description: When the traversal occurred.

schema.name: TriggerReason
schema.Description: Why this path rather than the default was taken.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI.

schema.name: RecipientId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one Recipients row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the Recipients row.

schema.name: DisplayName
schema.Description: Employee display name as carried in the consent registry.

schema.name: Organization
schema.Description: Organization the recipient belongs to.

schema.name: EmailAddress
schema.Description: Corporate email address; empty string when the recipient has no valid corporate email.

schema.name: MobileNumber
schema.Description: Mobile number in E.164 form; empty string when no mobile number is on file.

schema.name: SmsConsentStatus
schema.Description: Consent state as observed in the consent registry: Granted, Revoked, or NeverGiven.

schema.name: SmsConsentAt
schema.Description: Timestamp at which the current SMS consent state took effect.

schema.name: ConsentBinding
schema.Description: Operational binding through which this consent state was observed.

schema.name: HasSmsConsent
schema.Description: TRUE only when the recipient's SMS consent state is Granted.

schema.name: IsEmailReachable
schema.Description: TRUE when a corporate email address is on file for this recipient.

schema.name: IsSmsReachable
schema.Description: TRUE when a mobile number is on file for this recipient.

schema.name: IsUnreachable
schema.Description: TRUE when the recipient has neither an email address nor a mobile number -- the exc-unreachable trigger condition.

schema.name: IsCommunicationallyStranded
schema.Description: TRUE when every channel we hold for this person is either non-consenting or unreachable, so no lawful route exists at all.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension IRI; recipients are not a PKO 2.0.0 native class.

schema.name: MessageDeliveryId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one MessageDeliveries row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the MessageDeliveries row.

schema.name: ProcedureExecution
schema.Description: The procedure execution this send was performed under.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: The step execution that performed the send.

schema.name: Recipient
schema.Description: Who the message was sent to.

schema.name: MessageTemplate
schema.Description: Template that was rendered for this send.

schema.name: SentByAgent
schema.Description: Agent that performed the send.

schema.name: RenderedBody
schema.Description: The exact body text that was transmitted, after variable substitution.

schema.name: SentAt
schema.Description: Transmission timestamp.

schema.name: SentAtLocalHour
schema.Description: Hour of day 0-23 in the RECIPIENT's local timezone at transmission. Stored raw because recipient-local time is what the quiet-hours rule is written against and it is not derivable from SentAt alone.

schema.name: DeliveryStatus
schema.Description: Sent, Delivered, Failed, Suppressed, or Bounced.

schema.name: SuppressionReason
schema.Description: When DeliveryStatus is Suppressed, why; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: InvokedException
schema.Description: Documented exception invoked for this delivery, when one was.

schema.name: AcknowledgedAt
schema.Description: When the recipient acknowledged; null when not acknowledged.

schema.name: PolicyChannel
schema.Description: The communication policy governing the template that was sent.

schema.name: ChannelName
schema.Description: Channel (Email, SMS) of the policy this delivery went out under.

schema.name: PolicyRequiresConsent
schema.Description: Whether the governing policy requires active consent for this channel.

schema.name: RecipientHasSmsConsent
schema.Description: Whether the recipient of this delivery had SMS consent on file.

schema.name: WasActuallyTransmitted
schema.Description: TRUE when the message actually left our systems. Suppressed and Failed messages never reached the carrier.

schema.name: IsConsentViolation
schema.Description: TRUE when a message was actually transmitted on a consent-required channel to a recipient without consent. This is the TCPA witness.

schema.name: ConsentViolationPolicyKey
schema.Description: Carries the policy id only on rows that are consent violations; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: PolicyQuietHoursStartHour
schema.Description: Quiet-hours start hour (0-23) from the governing policy.

schema.name: PolicyQuietHoursEndHour
schema.Description: Quiet-hours end hour (0-23) from the governing policy.

schema.name: PolicyHasQuietHours
schema.Description: TRUE when the governing policy actually declares a quiet-hours window. comm-email-policy has 00:00-00:00, meaning no window.

schema.name: QuietWindowWrapsMidnight
schema.Description: TRUE when the quiet window crosses midnight (20:00 -> 08:00 does).

schema.name: IsInsideQuietWindow
schema.Description: TRUE when the recipient-local send hour falls inside the declared quiet window, handling the midnight wrap correctly.

schema.name: IsQuietHoursViolation
schema.Description: TRUE when a message was actually transmitted inside a declared quiet-hours window. The quiet-hours witness.

schema.name: QuietHoursViolationPolicyKey
schema.Description: Carries the policy id only on quiet-hours violations; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: RecipientIsUnreachable
schema.Description: Whether the recipient has neither email nor mobile on file -- the exc-unreachable trigger.

schema.name: IsAcknowledged
schema.Description: TRUE when an acknowledgement timestamp is recorded for this delivery.

schema.name: InvokedExceptionCondition
schema.Description: The documented condition of the exception invoked on this delivery, if any.

schema.name: HasUnreachableExceptionInvoked
schema.Description: TRUE when the documented unreachable-recipient exception was formally invoked on this delivery.

schema.name: IsFabricatedAcknowledgement
schema.Description: TRUE when an acknowledgement is recorded for a recipient we could not reach. This is the misrepresentation witness.

schema.name: IsUnhandledUnreachable
schema.Description: TRUE when a recipient was unreachable but no documented exception was invoked -- the case fell on the floor silently.

schema.name: UnreachableFailureKey
schema.Description: Carries the execution id on any unreachable-handling failure; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: PolicyRetentionDays
schema.Description: Retention commitment in days inherited from the governing channel policy.

schema.name: EvaluationContext
schema.Description: The evaluation context this row's time-dependent witnesses are judged under.

schema.name: AsOfInstant
schema.Description: The evaluation instant this row's time-dependent witnesses are judged against.

schema.name: AgeDays
schema.Description: Days elapsed since transmission.

schema.name: IsWithinRetentionWindow
schema.Description: TRUE while this delivery is still inside its committed retention period.

schema.name: HasRenderedBody
schema.Description: TRUE when the exact transmitted text is still held on this record.

schema.name: IsEvidenceRequired
schema.Description: TRUE when we are still obliged to hold this message's text.

schema.name: IsRetentionBreach
schema.Description: TRUE when we are obliged to hold the message text and do not. The evidentiary-gap witness.

schema.name: RetentionBreachExecutionKey
schema.Description: Carries the execution id on retention breaches; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: SendingStepExecutionStep
schema.Description: Which procedure step this delivery was performed under.

schema.name: ExecutionHasClearedLegalReview
schema.Description: Whether legal review had passed for the execution this delivery belongs to.

schema.name: IsUnreviewedSend
schema.Description: TRUE when a message was transmitted under an execution whose legal review had not passed. The ungated-send witness.

schema.name: RenderedBodyLength
schema.Description: Character length of the exact text that was actually transmitted.

schema.name: PolicyMaxMessageLengthAtSend
schema.Description: The per-segment limit that governed this specific send.

schema.name: SegmentCount
schema.Description: How many channel segments the transmitted text occupied.

schema.name: PolicyMaxSegmentsAtSend
schema.Description: The segment ceiling that governed this specific send.

schema.name: IsOverSegmentLimit
schema.Description: TRUE when a transmitted message split into more segments than the policy permits. The oversize witness.

schema.name: TemplateHasValidApproval
schema.Description: Whether the template used for this delivery had a properly-authorized approval.

schema.name: IsUnapprovedSend
schema.Description: TRUE when a message was transmitted using a template with no valid approval behind it.

schema.name: PolicyRequiredOptOutPhrase
schema.Description: The opt-out phrase required for this delivery's channel.

schema.name: PolicyRequiresOptOut
schema.Description: TRUE when the governing channel policy declares a required opt-out phrase at all.

schema.name: OptOutPhrasePosition
schema.Description: Character position at which the required opt-out phrase appears in the transmitted text; 0 when absent.

schema.name: HasOptOutPhrase
schema.Description: TRUE when the required opt-out phrase appears anywhere in the transmitted text.

schema.name: IsOptOutInFirstSegment
schema.Description: TRUE when the opt-out phrase falls inside the first segment, where it is guaranteed to be read.

schema.name: IsMissingRequiredOptOut
schema.Description: TRUE when a transmitted message on an opt-out-required channel did not contain the phrase at all. The hard witness.

schema.name: IsOptOutAtRiskOfTruncation
schema.Description: TRUE when the opt-out phrase is present but sits beyond the first segment, where carrier truncation or out-of-order delivery can hide it. The soft witness.

schema.name: IsFailedDelivery
schema.Description: TRUE when the message left our systems but did not land.

schema.name: IsSuppressed
schema.Description: TRUE when we deliberately chose not to transmit.

schema.name: IsTriaged
schema.Description: TRUE when some documented exception was formally invoked on this delivery -- i.e. a human or a rule picked it up.

schema.name: IsAbandonedFailure
schema.Description: TRUE when a delivery failed and no documented exception was invoked. The abandoned-bounce witness.

schema.name: AbandonedFailureExecutionKey
schema.Description: Carries the execution id on abandoned failures; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: ReachedExecutionKey
schema.Description: Carries the execution id on confirmed-delivered messages; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: TemplateWasSendable
schema.Description: Whether the template used for this delivery was fully sendable under a current, matching approval.

schema.name: IsDriftedSend
schema.Description: TRUE when a message was transmitted from a template that was not validly sendable at the time. The drift witness.

schema.name: DriftedSendTemplateKey
schema.Description: Carries the template id on drifted sends; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: WasSentOutsideBusinessHours
schema.Description: TRUE when this message landed before 08:00 or after 18:00 in the recipient's local time.

schema.name: WasDeliveredAndUnanswered
schema.Description: TRUE when a message actually reached someone and no acknowledgement came back.

schema.name: IsPoorlyTimedUnanswered
schema.Description: TRUE when an unanswered message was delivered outside business hours -- a timing hypothesis for the silence.

schema.name: IsWellTimedUnanswered
schema.Description: TRUE when a message was delivered at a reasonable hour and still drew no response.

schema.name: UnansweredTemplateKey
schema.Description: The template id when this delivery went unanswered, otherwise empty string.

schema.name: TransmittedTemplateKey
schema.Description: The template id when this delivery actually reached someone.

schema.name: ApprovingAgentAtSend
schema.Description: The agent whose approval authorized this specific transmission, frozen at send time.

schema.name: ApprovingRoleAtSend
schema.Description: The role that agent held when they approved, frozen at send time.

schema.name: ApprovalDecidedAtSend
schema.Description: When the relied-upon approval was granted, frozen at send time.

schema.name: ApprovalPrecededSend
schema.Description: TRUE when the approval we relied on was granted before the message went out.

schema.name: HasFrozenApprovalEvidence
schema.Description: TRUE when this delivery carries a complete frozen record of who authorized it and when.

schema.name: ProvenanceIsLiveDerived
schema.Description: TRUE when we have no frozen evidence and the only available answer comes from recomputing against today's template state.

schema.name: CurrentLastApprovalAt
schema.Description: The template's most recent approval time as it stands NOW, for comparison against what was approved at send.

schema.name: TemplateReapprovedSinceSend
schema.Description: TRUE when the template has picked up a newer approval since this message was transmitted.

schema.name: IsUnprovableApprovalClaim
schema.Description: TRUE when we assert this send was approved, hold no frozen evidence, and the template has been re-approved since. The claim cannot be substantiated from the record.

schema.name: ReminderSentAt
schema.Description: When I sent a follow-up reminder about this unacknowledged message. Empty when I never did.

schema.name: ReminderCount
schema.Description: How many reminders I have sent for this delivery.

schema.name: HasSentReminder
schema.Description: TRUE when at least one reminder went out for this delivery.

schema.name: AcknowledgementIsOutstanding
schema.Description: TRUE when a message actually reached someone, carried an acknowledgement obligation, and has not been acknowledged.

schema.name: OutstandingAgeDays
schema.Description: How long this acknowledgement has been outstanding.

schema.name: IsUnchasedAcknowledgement
schema.Description: TRUE when an acknowledgement has been outstanding for more than 7 days and I have never sent a reminder.

schema.name: IsExhaustedFollowUp
schema.Description: TRUE when I have sent three or more reminders and still have no acknowledgement -- the point at which this stops being my work and becomes a human escalation.

schema.name: NeedsHumanEscalation
schema.Description: TRUE when follow-up is exhausted and no exception has been invoked to close out the obligation.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension IRI; per-recipient delivery events are not a PKO 2.0.0 native class.

schema.name: TemplateApprovalId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one TemplateApprovals row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the TemplateApprovals row.

schema.name: MessageTemplate
schema.Description: Template this decision applies to.

schema.name: DecidedByAgent
schema.Description: Agent who made the approval decision.

schema.name: DecidedInRole
schema.Description: Role the agent was acting in when deciding.

schema.name: Decision
schema.Description: Approved, Rejected, or Withdrawn.

schema.name: DecidedAt
schema.Description: When the decision was recorded.

schema.name: ApprovedBodyHash
schema.Description: Digest of the exact template body that was approved, so later edits are detectable.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Approver's rationale or conditions.

schema.name: IsApprovalDecision
schema.Description: TRUE when this decision row is an approval rather than a rejection or withdrawal.

schema.name: TemplatePolicy
schema.Description: The communication policy governing the template being approved.

schema.name: RequiredApprovalRole
schema.Description: The role the governing policy designates as approver -- communications-manager for both current policies.

schema.name: IsDecidedByRequiredRole
schema.Description: TRUE when the approving role matches the role the policy designates.

schema.name: ValidApprovalTemplateKey
schema.Description: Carries the template id only on approvals made by the correct role; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension IRI; template approval events are not a PKO 2.0.0 native class.

data.Notes: Reviewed against comm-email-policy required content and retention.
Approved against comm-sms-policy: 160-char limit, 3-segment cap, required STOP opt-out, 20:00-08:00 quiet window.

schema.name: SendIntentId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one SendIntents row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the SendIntents row.

schema.name: ProcedureExecution
schema.Description: Execution this send intent belongs to.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: Step execution evaluating this intent.

schema.name: Recipient
schema.Description: Intended recipient.

schema.name: MessageTemplate
schema.Description: Template the pipeline intends to render and send.

schema.name: ProposedBody
schema.Description: The fully rendered text the pipeline intends to transmit, before transmission.

schema.name: ProposedSendAtLocalHour
schema.Description: Hour 0-23 in the recipient's local timezone at which the pipeline intends to transmit.

schema.name: EvaluatedAt
schema.Description: When the pipeline evaluated the gates for this intent.

schema.name: ResultingDelivery
schema.Description: The delivery record produced from this intent, whether transmitted or suppressed.

schema.name: IntentPolicy
schema.Description: The communication policy governing the template this intent would send.

schema.name: IntentChannel
schema.Description: Channel this intent would transmit on.

schema.name: PolicyIsActive
schema.Description: Whether the governing policy is currently Active rather than Draft, Suspended, or Retired.

schema.name: IntentRequiresConsent
schema.Description: Whether the governing policy requires consent for this channel.

schema.name: RecipientHasChannelConsent
schema.Description: Whether the intended recipient has active SMS consent on record.

schema.name: ConsentGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when either the channel does not require consent, or the recipient has granted it.

schema.name: RecipientIsSmsReachable
schema.Description: Whether a mobile number is on file for the intended recipient.

schema.name: RecipientIsEmailReachable
schema.Description: Whether an email address is on file for the intended recipient.

schema.name: ReachabilityGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when the recipient is reachable on the channel this intent would use.

schema.name: PermissionGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when the policy is active, consent is satisfied, and the recipient is reachable on this channel. The channel-permission gate.

schema.name: IntentQuietStartHour
schema.Description: Quiet-hours start hour from the governing policy.

schema.name: IntentQuietEndHour
schema.Description: Quiet-hours end hour from the governing policy.

schema.name: IntentPolicyHasQuietHours
schema.Description: TRUE when the governing policy declares a real quiet-hours window.

schema.name: IntentQuietWindowWraps
schema.Description: TRUE when the quiet window crosses midnight.

schema.name: IntentIsInsideQuietWindow
schema.Description: TRUE when the proposed recipient-local send hour falls inside the forbidden window.

schema.name: TimingGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when transmitting now is permitted on timing grounds. The timing gate.

schema.name: HoursUntilWindowOpens
schema.Description: How many hours the pipeline must defer before transmission becomes permitted; 0 when already permitted.

schema.name: IntentMaxMessageLength
schema.Description: Per-segment character limit from the governing policy.

schema.name: IntentMaxSegments
schema.Description: Segment ceiling from the governing policy.

schema.name: ProposedBodyLength
schema.Description: Character count of ProposedBody, written by the pipeline at render time.

schema.name: ProposedSegmentCount
schema.Description: Number of channel segments ProposedBody will occupy, computed by the pipeline at render time.

schema.name: LengthGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when the proposed text is non-empty and fits within the segment ceiling.

schema.name: IntentRequiredOptOutPhrase
schema.Description: Opt-out phrase the governing policy requires in every message on this channel.

schema.name: ProposedOptOutPosition
schema.Description: Character position of the required opt-out phrase within ProposedBody; 0 when absent. Written by the pipeline at render time.

schema.name: OptOutGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when no opt-out is required, or the required phrase is present within the first segment.

schema.name: ContentGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE when the proposed text satisfies every content rule of the governing channel policy.

schema.name: TemplateIsSendable
schema.Description: Whether the template carries a current, properly-authorized, non-drifted approval.

schema.name: ExecutionHasLegalClearance
schema.Description: Whether legal review has passed for the execution this intent belongs to.

schema.name: IntentApprovalRole
schema.Description: The role the governing policy designates as the approving authority.

schema.name: ApprovalRoleAgentKind
schema.Description: Whether the agent currently filling the designated approval role is Human, AIAgent, or AutomatedPipeline.

schema.name: ApprovalIsHuman
schema.Description: TRUE when the designated approval role is currently held by a human agent.

schema.name: AuthorizationGatePassed
schema.Description: TRUE only when the template is validly approved, legal review has cleared, and the approving role is held by a human. The authorization gate.

schema.name: IsClearedToSend
schema.Description: TRUE only when all four gates pass. THE single column the pipeline reads before transmitting.

schema.name: BlockingGateName
schema.Description: Names the first gate that refused, for the suppression reason; empty string when cleared.

schema.name: HasResultingDelivery
schema.Description: TRUE when this intent produced a delivery record of any status.

schema.name: ResultingDeliveryWasTransmitted
schema.Description: Whether the delivery produced from this intent actually left our systems.

schema.name: IsOverriddenRefusal
schema.Description: TRUE when a gate refused and the message was transmitted regardless. The override witness.

schema.name: IsSilentlyDropped
schema.Description: TRUE when a gate refused and no delivery record of any kind was produced -- the recipient vanished from the run.

schema.name: ResultingDeliveryException
schema.Description: The documented exception invoked on the delivery produced from this intent, if any.

schema.name: RefusalCitedAnException
schema.Description: TRUE when the suppression produced by this refusal cited a documented exception.

schema.name: IsProperlyHandledRefusal
schema.Description: TRUE when a refusal correctly resulted in a non-transmitted delivery record citing a documented exception. The positive witness.

schema.name: RefusalFailureExecutionKey
schema.Description: Carries the execution id on any mishandled refusal; empty string otherwise.

schema.name: IntentExecutionKey
schema.Description: The procedure execution id for every intent, used as the campaign rollup key.

schema.name: DeliveredIntentExecutionKey
schema.Description: The execution id when this intent actually reached a person, otherwise empty string.

schema.name: DroppedIntentExecutionKey
schema.Description: The execution id when this intent was refused and left no record at all.

schema.name: MyApprovalWasInForce
schema.Description: TRUE when the template carried a valid approval at the moment this intent was evaluated.

schema.name: RefusedOnApprovedContent
schema.Description: TRUE when the pipeline refused an intent on content grounds even though the template was approved.

schema.name: RefusedOnOptOutOnly
schema.Description: TRUE when the only content failure was a missing or mispositioned opt-out phrase.

schema.name: RefusalWasOnMyRules
schema.Description: TRUE when the refusal came from a communications rule I own -- content, length, opt-out, quiet hours.

schema.name: RefusalWasOutsideMyControl
schema.Description: TRUE when the refusal came from consent, reachability, or authorization -- none of which I can fix by editing a template.

schema.name: ApproverWasNotified
schema.Description: Whether the approving human was informed that this intent was refused.

schema.name: IsUnreportedRefusalOnMyRules
schema.Description: TRUE when a refusal I own and could have fixed was never surfaced to me.

schema.name: IsApprovalOverriddenSilently
schema.Description: TRUE when the pipeline overrode a valid human approval on content grounds and told nobody.

schema.name: AlternateChannelIntent
schema.Description: The follow-up intent raised on a different channel after this one was refused. Stored as a raw identifier rather than an FK: SendIntents pointing at SendIntents would make the table self-referential in a way the DAG contract does not allow here.

schema.name: HasAlternateChannelAttempt
schema.Description: TRUE when a follow-up intent on another channel was raised for this refused send.

schema.name: AlternateAttemptWasCleared
schema.Description: Whether the follow-up intent itself passed all gates.

schema.name: IsRefusedWithNoAlternative
schema.Description: TRUE when a send was refused and no attempt was ever made on any other channel.

schema.name: ExceptionPrescribedAnAlternative
schema.Description: TRUE when the documented exception for this refusal prescribes a different-channel send as the correct handling.

schema.name: PrescribedHandlingWasPerformed
schema.Description: TRUE when the exception prescribed an alternate channel and an alternate intent was actually raised and cleared.

schema.name: IsSuppressionWithoutRemedy
schema.Description: TRUE when we cited an exception that prescribed an alternate channel and then never performed it.

schema.name: RefusalRecordedAt
schema.Description: When this refusal was written to a durable record. Empty when no record was ever emitted.

schema.name: RefusalNotifiedRole
schema.Description: The role informed that this send was refused.

schema.name: HasDurableRefusalRecord
schema.Description: TRUE when this refusal was written down somewhere a human can find it.

schema.name: RefusalWasEscalated
schema.Description: TRUE when a specific role was notified of this refusal.

schema.name: IsUnrecordedRefusal
schema.Description: TRUE when I refused a send and produced no delivery record, no refusal record, and no exception. The refusal left no trace of any kind.

schema.name: IsUnescalatedRefusal
schema.Description: TRUE when a refusal was recorded but no human role was ever told.

schema.name: UnescalatedRefusalRoleKey
schema.Description: Echoes the role that should have been told about this refusal, but only when the refusal went unrecorded. Empty otherwise.

schema.name: UnrecordedRefusalExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent procedure execution only for refusals nobody recorded; empty otherwise.

schema.name: RetryIntent
schema.Description: The intent I raised when the quiet window reopened for this deferred send. Raw identifier rather than an FK: a SendIntents self-reference is not expressible as a relationship without making the table depend on itself.

schema.name: WasDeferredOnTiming
schema.Description: TRUE when the timing gate is the reason this intent did not clear, and every other gate passed.

schema.name: EvaluationContext
schema.Description: The evaluation context this row's time-dependent witnesses are judged under.

schema.name: AsOfInstant
schema.Description: The evaluation instant this row's time-dependent witnesses are judged against.

schema.name: WindowHasSinceReopened
schema.Description: TRUE when enough time has passed since evaluation that the quiet window this intent hit must have closed.

schema.name: HasRetryAttempt
schema.Description: TRUE when a retry intent was raised for this deferred send.

schema.name: RetryWasCleared
schema.Description: Whether the retry intent itself passed all gates.

schema.name: IsAbandonedDeferral
schema.Description: TRUE when a send was deferred for timing, the window has since reopened, and no retry was ever raised. A deferral silently converted into a cancellation.

schema.name: DeferralAgeHours
schema.Description: How long this deferred intent has been sitting since evaluation.

schema.name: IsStaleDeferral
schema.Description: TRUE when a deferred send has been waiting more than 24 hours -- longer than any quiet window can justify.

schema.name: EvaluatingRoleAssignment
schema.Description: The RoleAssignments id under which the pipeline evaluated this send intent. Raw rather than an FK because SendIntents already sits downstream of the execution graph and an added edge to RoleAssignments is not needed to resolve it.

schema.name: EnforcedByUnauthorizedAgent
schema.Description: Whether the gate decision on this intent was made by an agent holding an unauthorized enforcement assignment.

schema.name: ConsentInputWasResolvable
schema.Description: TRUE when the recipient's consent state was actually retrievable, as opposed to absent and read as a refusal.

schema.name: RecipientConsentStatusRaw
schema.Description: The recipient's consent status as a string: Granted, Revoked, NeverGiven, or empty if no consent record exists at all.

schema.name: PolicyInputWasResolvable
schema.Description: TRUE when a governing communication policy was actually found for this intent.

schema.name: AllGateInputsResolved
schema.Description: TRUE when every input my gates depend on was actually retrievable.

schema.name: IsUnevaluableRefusal
schema.Description: TRUE when I refused a send but at least one gate input could not be resolved -- so I do not actually know whether the rule was violated or merely unreadable.

schema.name: GateResultWasIndependentlyConfirmed
schema.Description: Whether any agent other than me verified this gate outcome.

schema.name: IsSelfWitnessedDecision
schema.Description: TRUE when the entire decision to send or refuse rests solely on my own computation, unconfirmed by anything else.

schema.name: IsIndependentlyConfirmed
schema.Description: TRUE when this intent's decision was corroborated by an actual delivery record rather than resting solely on the pipeline's own say-so.

schema.name: IndependentlyConfirmedExecutionKey
schema.Description: Echoes the parent execution only for independently confirmed intents; empty otherwise.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension IRI; pre-send intents are not a PKO 2.0.0 native class.

schema.name: AgentDecisionRecordId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one AgentDecisionRecords row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the AgentDecisionRecords row.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: Step execution during which the decision was made.

schema.name: DecidingAgent
schema.Description: Agent that made the decision.

schema.name: DecisionKind
schema.Description: Classification, Prioritization, Draft, Suppression, Escalation, or Posting.

schema.name: DecisionSummary
schema.Description: What the agent decided, in one sentence.

schema.name: DecidedAt
schema.Description: When the decision was made.

schema.name: MaterialityBand
schema.Description: BelowThreshold, Material, or Escalated.

schema.name: HumanDisposition
schema.Description: Accepted, Corrected, Reversed, or NotReviewed.

schema.name: ReviewedByAgent
schema.Description: Human agent that dispositioned the decision.

schema.name: ReviewedAt
schema.Description: When a human dispositioned the decision.

schema.name: WasOverridden
schema.Description: TRUE when a human corrected or reversed this decision.

schema.name: WasReviewed
schema.Description: TRUE when a human actually dispositioned this decision.

schema.name: DecidingAgentKind
schema.Description: Whether the deciding agent is Human, AIAgent, or AutomatedPipeline.

schema.name: DecidingAgentWhenOverridden
schema.Description: Echoes the deciding agent id when the decision was overridden, blank otherwise.

schema.name: UnderRoleAssignment
schema.Description: Role assignment in force when the decision was made; anchors the decision to one side of a handover.

schema.name: RoleAssignmentWhenScored
schema.Description: Echoes the governing role assignment when one is recorded, blank otherwise.

schema.name: RoleAssignmentWhenOverridden
schema.Description: Echoes the governing role assignment when the decision was overridden, blank otherwise.

schema.name: StepOfDecision
schema.Description: The specified step at which this decision was made.

schema.name: BoundaryMatchKey
schema.Description: Composite key of step, deciding agent kind, and decision kind for this decision.

schema.name: MatchingBoundaryCount
schema.Description: Number of authority boundaries this decision matches.

schema.name: ViolatedAuthorityBoundary
schema.Description: TRUE when this decision matches an authority boundary that forbids it.

schema.name: ReviewerAgentKind
schema.Description: Agent kind of the reviewer, if any.

schema.name: HasHumanConfirmation
schema.Description: TRUE when a human agent actually dispositioned this decision.

schema.name: NeedsHumanConfirmation
schema.Description: TRUE when a non-human agent made a material or escalated decision.

schema.name: IsUnconfirmedNonHumanDecision
schema.Description: TRUE when a material non-human decision was never confirmed by a human.

schema.name: StepExecutionWhenUnconfirmed
schema.Description: Echoes the step-execution id when the decision is an unconfirmed non-human material decision, blank otherwise.

schema.name: AgentWhenBoundaryViolated
schema.Description: Echoes the deciding agent id when the decision violated a boundary, blank otherwise.

schema.name: ReviewLatencyMinutes
schema.Description: Minutes between the decision and its human disposition; 0 when never reviewed. Declared number, not integer: DATETIME_DIFF returns a numeric and an integer cast makes the entire view fail on read.

schema.name: IsDraftKind
schema.Description: TRUE when this decision produced text — the drafter's own output class.

schema.name: AgentWhenDraftOverridden
schema.Description: Echoes the deciding agent id when a drafting decision was overridden, blank otherwise.

schema.name: AgentWhenDraft
schema.Description: Echoes the deciding agent id when the decision produced text, blank otherwise.

schema.name: OverrideReasonKind
schema.Description: Why the human changed my output: ErrorCorrection, JudgmentReserved, PolicyChange, or empty when not overridden.

schema.name: IsErrorCorrection
schema.Description: TRUE when the override corrected something I got wrong.

schema.name: IsReservedJudgmentOverride
schema.Description: TRUE when the override was a human exercising authority the procedure always reserved to them.

schema.name: OverrideReasonIsRecorded
schema.Description: TRUE when an override carries a stated reason.

schema.name: IsUnexplainedOverride
schema.Description: TRUE when my output was changed and nobody recorded why.

schema.name: ErrorCorrectionRoleAssignmentKey
schema.Description: The role assignment id when this decision was overridden as an error correction, otherwise empty.

schema.name: BoundaryViolationRoleAssignmentKey
schema.Description: Echoes the role assignment this decision was made under, but only when the decision violated an authority boundary. Empty otherwise.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI for an agent decision record.

data.DecisionSummary: Posted approved close entries and published the package.
Accepted a trial-balance extract in which one feed was 17 minutes old.

DeliveredCommunications.Description: Everything above is a proxy for the real question, which is instance-level: THIS message, to THIS recipient, rendered from THIS template, authorized by THIS approval. The model has MessageTemplates and CommunicationPolicies as specifications and no record of a single thing ever sent. Without an instance table, 'can I show that what was sent matched what I approved' is permanently unanswerable rather than merely unanswered — and it is the question a disputing employee actually asks. Extension term (urn:effortless:pko-extension#DeliveredCommunication); PKO has no native class for a delivered artifact instance.

schema.name: DeliveredCommunicationId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one DeliveredCommunications row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias.

schema.name: ProcedureExecution
schema.Description: The run that produced this delivery.

schema.name: SendingStepExecution
schema.Description: The policy-07 step execution that sent it.

schema.name: AuthorizingStepExecution
schema.Description: The human approval gate execution that authorized this content. Null means nobody approved it.

schema.name: MessageTemplate
schema.Description: The approved template this delivery was rendered from.

schema.name: Channel
schema.Description: Email or SMS.

schema.name: RecipientKey
schema.Description: Opaque recipient identifier; no personal data in the rulebook.

schema.name: SentAt
schema.Description: When the message was dispatched.

schema.name: RenderedContentHash
schema.Description: Hash of the exact bytes delivered, for comparison against the approved rendering.

schema.name: ApprovedContentHash
schema.Description: Hash of the content as it stood at the approval gate.

schema.name: DeliveryStatus
schema.Description: Provider-reported delivery outcome.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI.

schema.name: HasAuthorization
schema.Description: TRUE when this delivery names the approval that authorized it.

schema.name: ContentMatchesApproval
schema.Description: TRUE when the bytes delivered are the bytes approved.

schema.name: AuthorizedAt
schema.Description: When the authorizing approval completed.

schema.name: WasApprovedBeforeSending
schema.Description: TRUE when the approval preceded the send.

schema.name: IsDefensible
schema.Description: TRUE when this delivery can be defended in a dispute: authorized, unaltered, and approved beforehand.

schema.name: AuthorityBoundaryId
schema.Description: Stored logical identifier for one AuthorityBoundaries row.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the AuthorityBoundaries row.

schema.name: Step
schema.Description: Step at which the boundary applies.

schema.name: ForbiddenAgentKind
schema.Description: Agent kind that may not perform the forbidden decision kind: Human, AIAgent, or AutomatedPipeline.

schema.name: ForbiddenDecisionKind
schema.Description: Decision kind forbidden to that agent kind at this step.

schema.name: RatifiedByKnowledgeFragment
schema.Description: Knowledge fragment that states the boundary.

schema.name: EnforcingRequirement
schema.Description: Requirement under which the boundary is enforced, if any.

schema.name: AuthorityRole
schema.Description: Role accountable for the boundary.

schema.name: ValidFrom
schema.Description: Start of the boundary's valid-time interval.

schema.name: ValidTo
schema.Description: End of the boundary's valid-time interval; null means open-ended.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Approved, Proposed, or Retired.

schema.name: EvaluationContext
schema.Description: The evaluation context this boundary's currency is judged under.

schema.name: AsOfInstant
schema.Description: The evaluation instant this boundary is judged against.

schema.name: IsCurrentlyBinding
schema.Description: TRUE when this boundary is approved and inside its valid-time window right now.

schema.name: RatifyingFragmentIsValid
schema.Description: Whether the knowledge fragment that ratified this boundary is still valid.

schema.name: StepWhenBinding
schema.Description: Echoes the step id when this boundary is currently binding, blank otherwise.

schema.name: BoundaryMatchKey
schema.Description: Composite key of step, forbidden agent kind, and forbidden decision kind.

schema.name: ViolationCount
schema.Description: Number of recorded decisions that this boundary forbids.

schema.name: IsUntested
schema.Description: TRUE when a binding boundary has never been triggered by any recorded decision.

schema.name: HasRatifyingFragment
schema.Description: TRUE when this boundary names a knowledge fragment as its justification. FALSE means the rule constrains behaviour on nobody's recorded authority — strictly worse than resting on an expired claim, and previously invisible because the ratification lookup returned NULL.

schema.name: IsUnwarranted
schema.Description: TRUE when a binding constraint on authority rests on no ratifying claim at all, or on one that is no longer valid. Either way the rule is being enforced without a live justification.

schema.name: RatifyingFragmentIsOverdue
schema.Description: Whether the fragment ratifying this boundary is past its review date.

schema.name: RatifyingFragmentIsSingleWitness
schema.Description: Whether the fragment ratifying this boundary rests on a single witness.

schema.name: WarrantIsThin
schema.Description: A binding boundary whose ratifying knowledge is either overdue for review or single-sourced — still valid, but weakly warranted.

schema.name: IsUnwarrantedAndUntested
schema.Description: A boundary whose ratification has lapsed and which no agent decision has ever been evaluated against — we cannot show it works and we cannot show why it exists.

schema.name: UnwarrantedBoundaryStepKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: the step this boundary governs when the boundary is unwarranted, blank otherwise.

schema.name: RatifyingFragmentKey
schema.Description: Composite-key echo: the fragment ratifying this boundary when the boundary is currently binding, blank otherwise.

schema.name: RatifyingFragmentStatus
schema.Description: The status string of the knowledge fragment that ratifies this boundary: Approved, Reviewed, Draft, or empty.

schema.name: RatificationLapsed
schema.Description: TRUE when this boundary names a ratifying fragment and that fragment is no longer valid.

schema.name: BindsDespiteLapsedRatification
schema.Description: TRUE when a boundary is still enforced against agents while the knowledge that authorized it has lapsed.

schema.name: IsUngroundedAndUntested
schema.Description: TRUE when a boundary has lapsed ratification AND has never once been exercised -- so neither its authority nor its operation has ever been demonstrated.

schema.name: ConstrainedRoleAssignmentKey
schema.Description: The role id this boundary constrains, emitted only when the boundary is ungrounded.

schema.name: SemanticTypeIri
schema.Description: Extension class IRI for an authority boundary.

schema.name: BindingObservationId
schema.Description: Primary key of an execution-side observation of one operational binding, frozen at the moment a step actually ran.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the BindingObservations row.

schema.name: StepExecution
schema.Description: The step execution during which this binding was read.

schema.name: OperationalBinding
schema.Description: The spec-side binding this observation instantiates.

schema.name: ObservedSourceTimestamp
schema.Description: The LastObservedAt value the source actually carried at run time, captured then and never recomputed.

schema.name: ReadAt
schema.Description: When the step execution actually read this binding.

schema.name: SlaMinutesAtRun
schema.Description: The freshness SLA declared for this binding.

schema.name: AgeAtRunMinutes
schema.Description: How old the source data was at the instant the step read it.

schema.name: WasStaleAtRun
schema.Description: TRUE when an authoritative source was already outside its SLA at the moment the step consumed it.

schema.name: IsAuthoritativeBinding
schema.Description: Whether the binding observed is the authoritative source for its record key.

schema.name: StaleAtRunStepKey
schema.Description: Echoes the step execution id when the source was stale at run time.

schema.name: AttestationId
schema.Description: Primary key of a signature event: one person, one execution, one instant.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Human-readable calculated display alias for the Attestations row.

schema.name: ProcedureExecution
schema.Description: The execution being attested to.

schema.name: SignedByAgent
schema.Description: The human who signed.

schema.name: SignedAt
schema.Description: The instant of signature.

schema.name: AssuranceGradeAtSigning
schema.Description: The AssuranceGrade string as the model reported it at the moment of signature, captured then and never recomputed.

schema.name: VersionWasFitAtSigning
schema.Description: Whether the executed procedure version was fit to execute at the moment of signature.

schema.name: VersionIsFitNow
schema.Description: Whether the executed version reads as fit today.

schema.name: FitnessVerdictHasDrifted
schema.Description: TRUE when the fitness of the signed version reads differently today than it did at signature.

schema.name: AssuranceGradeNow
schema.Description: The AssuranceGrade the model reports for this execution today.

schema.name: AssuranceGradeHasDrifted
schema.Description: TRUE when the assurance behind this signature is described differently now than it was at signature.

schema.name: WouldNotSurviveRestatement
schema.Description: TRUE when re-deriving this attestation today would not reproduce what the model said when it was signed.
